content: I've been using this app for over a month now. It's fantastic. I've lost 9lbs. The system works if you stick to it. If you're the kind of person that is motivated by visual tools and data, this is for you! A small food scale is helpful (Amazon for like $8). Foods that I had been eating previously were healthy, whole foods, but my portions were too large, and my "treats" were more calorie dense than I realized. This app raises awareness about what you're putting in your body. Highly recommend!

Out of all of the similar apps out there, this is my favorite. I always have trouble with other apps and it's barcode scanning and, adding meals. This is the only app that I can actually add foods into a meal instead of logging each and every food separately. It also easily gives me suggestions and let's me search for foods. I also enjoy the mini challenges, such as going without chocolate for 21 days. It really keeps me motivated. It has a lot of features that other apps just don't have.

Outstanding app, even better than MyFitnessPal. Only wish I have is that it could add a collapsed view of the grocery list, i.e. if two recipes need chicken breast, just list the entire amount once, so I have a better overview when buying groceries. And this app is not just about weight loss - you care about healthy living, give this app a shot. It's amazing.

I was not sure about this application at first because of the price. I don't like paying out money for subscriptions but with some natural supplements and guidance from my chiropractor, I have totally changed my mind. This app has everything, water, fasting, tracking, exercise integration! So much easier to focus on a plan and make it work for you. I will be subscribing again next year!

I've only used Yazio for one day and I already love it. Pros:no intrusive ads, its not only a calorie counter but a supportive coach, the water tracker motivates you throughout the day with messages that you'll want to reach your goal. It seamlessly syncs with Google fit so that calories burned transfer over to your meal plan. Cons: Food database needs work. Many food choices once scanned were not correct. But you are able to add the correct food item to the database.

It is easy to record foods because so many people use this. But there are a lot of bad entries. I've put a few in, myself, good and bad, so one must be careful when sorting out which food applies or is accurate. I wish I could remove my bad-oops entries. I think these happen because we (I) don't understand what it's asking for and just want the calories, carb, fat, protein numbers--never mind the rest-- I want to get on with my day. I like the recipies, water tracker and weight tracker.

the best calorie tracking app I've tried, the premium price is great compared to other apps which also makes you want to stick to it more. only thing I wish was different is that you log the supplements/medicine as breakfast lunch or dinner and there isn't a different similar section for them, which I guess isn't a big issue but sometimes I kind of want to check back to see if I took them having them in a tab would make it easier. it also doesn't connect to Xiaomi bands which is an inconvenience

I've always been a little sceptical about such apps, but I guess I'm a stereotypical dude who decided to try it out and got nicely surprised. So far, I managed to lose 4 kg (8.8 lbs) in about 2 months. Of course, the app requires consequence. You need to be committed. Additionally, I run on the treadmill every other day. Still, the app is of great help. What I would personally improve is the possibility of editing the weight of meals scanned with the help of AI, not just the nutrients.

Absolutely wonderful! I needed a low-effort but precise app for calorie and protein counting, and I got one. Happily paid for premium. Barcode scanner and AI are beyond helpful, and recipes are great! Keep up the great work! P.S. Almost forgot to add - looove the little suggestions I get after logging the meal!

This app was the only one that kept me accountable for tracking my nutrition. I have been using it for only a month, but i cant deny that it has helped me tremendously to stay on track. - It is easy to use - the bar code scanner works smoothly - the AI aspect is 80% accurate (better than i expected!) -the macro-micronutrient stat's are easy to follow along. - solid nutrition tips Overall, a great app if you use the premium version (85% off sale for yearly subscription is worth it!).

Just hit my 90 day mark and I'm down 16.7 pounds! I absolutely LOVE this app, it's been a very important tool in my health journey helping you make sure you're hitting protein goals and staying under calories. I highly recommend this to anyone trying to eat healthier or lose weight to become healthier.

Edit: Fixed since today's update. Great! Since the last update I'm not able to choose any other language other than the system language. Before I was able to choose any language. Maybe you look into this. There is no other app on my device denying to change languages. Will update review when this works again.

I have been using Yazio for a few days now and it's the easiest food tracking app I've ever used. The AI picture tool is fantastic; you can adjust whatever was detected in easy text form. What I am missing is the option to edit a meal once it's logged, e.g. instead of 1 cup of rice to 1.5 cups etc. I would also like if I could "tell" the app that I am training for a half marathon on date xy, and that I train every Tuesday, Thursday and Sunday and thus need more carbs the day before

I USED TO HATE THIS APP!!! I originally gave it a 1 star review because one of the updates were genuinely horrible 😭 BUT I actually really like it now. It's super stimulating and rewarding, so it doesn't feel really boring or forgettable. It's easy to use and understand. And premium is honestly 100% worthit, I got it for $15 AUD and it's amazing. The only con I can think of is the fasting system is annoying. I'd use a different app for tracking fasts, but other than that it's amazing!!!

Even if everyone says the App doesn't work or whatever, I was sceptical but wanted to try it. It's amazing! AI is working very well and you have a perfect overview of what you had in a day, nutrients, exercises and calories. It helps a lot to keep track of ur weight.

This app has been great in the past for losing weight and still is, I preferred the way the old version looked. The heavy black theme is hard to look at for me, it'd be nice if it could be changed independent of the system's settings. Good app overall 👍

It works great, and it's pretty cheap compared to other apps out there. It helps to be mindful of what you eat, and if you also want to try intermediate fasting, it is like 2 in one app. Love it and highly recommend it if you want to start a new healthy lifestyle.

I really love this app - it has made my weight loss journey so much easier and more enjoyable! As a long-time and paying user, I just have one small request: could you please consider bringing back the previous app icon or logo? I can't quite explain why, but the new one feels a bit less motivating to me. The old design had brighter colors and felt more lively and inspiring 🙄🫣 and one more thing, I can't find overview of my weight loss. I could see the overview over months etc but now nothing.
One of crazy app in the world. love it. the only thing is missing they must bring workout plans in the app like others.

Great app, really liked it. Made my week streak, and I'm going to continue. It would be great to make a recipe filter feature, something like "What I have in my fridge" because a lot of recipes require specific products that can't be purchased in local small groceries and I need to plan my tomorrow breakfast. Thank you.

I love this app! I'm over 10kg down and it actually feels healthy and sustainable. However, a few things I would like to see: - A way to directly access my created meals/recipes through a separate tab at the bottom instead of going through adding them to a meal - Changing ingredients in recipes shouldn't retroactively change calories on old entries of said recipe. Just because I now use different cream doesn't mean I did last month. - a way to turn off the new meal tips - the old logo back🙈

I like it. it's really helping me. It's easy to use, the recipes look so good. I got ahead and paid the subscription. I don't regret it. I started using this app after an argument with my boss. It's funny because how I eat and what I eat doesn't have a single thing to do with that argument. Even so, I'm enjoying it. It's helping me to put on track my diet, something I was overlooking for a long time and I was avoiding doing. I'm starting to eat healthier and I feel happy about that.
I am so grateful for a free app to count calories. Makes it all the morw easier to stay slim.
It would be good to have a separate menu item where I can manage my own recipes and dishes, because currently I have to open one of the “add” menu options to do this.
Wow! I keep being surprised by the practical and handy tools this app offers. The buddies functionality is also really nice!! Easily the best tracker available.

Love this app! It's super simple to track your calories & has everything you can think of built in. When you search/scan your meals, its accurate & calories + nutrition facts are all accurate. There's a reminder for water intake throughout the day which is easy to log h2o intake. You can sync your smart watch & other fitness apps to Yazio. There are thousands of healthy recipes to choose from if you want to cook at home & 100s of workout routines to try. Free is great but Premium is the way2go

I totally love the app! But today I opened it and it had a new design, which just doesn't look good in my opinion... the old one was way better and actually pleasing to the eye. But except for that, it has nice features and it's easy to track calories and water intake. All in all, I love it!

Best App Experience! I'm A Pro User. But I Want Some Help From You Guys. I Asked AI About This Hope It'll Add In Future. Maybe In 6 Months Window I Really Want To See That Data Before My Annual Subscription Ends! Does Yazio Track EAA Intake? No, Yazio does not track essential amino acids (EAAs) like leucine directly. It focuses on calories, macros (protein/carbs/fats), micronutrients (like vitamins/minerals), and overall meal logging, but not detailed amino acid breakdown.

Why did you guy remove such a crucial feature like body measurements tracker? It's just a simple feature that really make me decided to subscribe! I even bought a weight scale that show %fat and %muscle to note in the app. I really want to refund and move to other app so bad 😟 Edit : Tysm for bringing back measurement logging. This is a great tiny feature that really important to me.
It's a great app to use. Fun, easy and worth every penny. My doctor actually told me that it is one of the best apps to use.

I KID YOU NOT, this app is THE BEST!!! I've been using it for like three months, and guess what? I’ve actually lost some weight! 💪 Even though the changes are kinda small right now, I know they’re gonna add up over time. Can’t wait to see where this journey takes me! 😎✨

I use this app for years and it's amazing, always improving and the free version is totally enough for calorie tracking and user friendly the whole app. Of course the free version is capable of so much other things too, but these are related to calorie tracking. I don't know what the pro version is like, but based on the free version I bet that's worth it. If you new to the calorie counting and you want to lose weight the pro version probably makes easier to get your goals. Best C.C. apps!

one of the best apps on the market but there is still room for improvement. 1. If I want to enter some custom meal sometimes I would prefer to enter nutritional value per 100g and then enter how much I ate. 2. Amount of this redundant popup is crazy 3. You should add some competition with friends. I cannot see what my buddies ate or how was their previous day. That is ruining whole feature

I've lost over a stone in the 100 days I've used the app, simply by logging my food intake and activity. It took the hard work and tedium out of eating healthily and sensibly, and helped me control portion sizes without going hungry

Pretty good app. It exceeds my expectations. i wanted something simple what will give me calories for my meals, but this keeps record of my meals and accounts for excersize as well. I am impressed with the variety of meals in the database, i'm from Slovakia and ate knedlik as part of the dinner and just for fun i looked it up and it was there! connectivity with other apps is great as well, automatically gets the data of fitness apps which is great! Great job guys! I get PRO as soon as I can!

Seems like a good app, too bad that you can only access the most interesting features after paying. They don't even offer a free trial period to see if this is indeed something for you. EDIT: After using this app for some time, including the pro version for the trial period offered, my main takeaway is that this is the best app of its kind currently available. The food database is impressive and adding stuff is very easy. The nutrient content could use some more work, as it is incomplete.

Absolutely love this app. It helps me keep track of my caloric intake and is so easy to use. With all of it's uses it helps you stay on track and gives you all the information you need to reach your goals. 10/10.

I am using it to monitor and control my daily Calories, Protein, Carbs and Fats intake. It lets you select the foods you have taken during the day from vast selection of food lists to search from. Then gives a quick summary of where you stand in terms of calorie intake etc. Makes achieving your goal; weight loss, muscle gain, diet monitoring etc much more convenient.. an effective app which is easy to use.

i used it last summer and within 2 weeks ive lost 5kg. i love it! it made me more confident and happy and all it took its 5 minutes or less to put in my meals. absolutely great maybe a little bit to much pushing me to buy pro version but everything else, great

I've been using the free version of this app primarily as a calorie counter for a couple months now and I love it. Lovely design and it really isn't that time consuming to make your own entries as other reviewer's have complained about. Only feature that could make it better for my friend and I would be a way to add eachother and share goals etc.

Amazing! This has everything i actually need, i can keep track of my calories (with the estimates, ofc), there's intermittent fasting, goals, notes and more. What i appreciated the most was the fact that i was able to sync Samsung Health with the app, it's all seamless really. The notifications are also a nice touch. The price per year might be a little too costly for a lot of people at first, but when you take into account the amount of things the app provides, it ends up really being worth it

Awesome app! I honestly don't know which feature I like best. I haven't used a similar app before, having all features in one app. I was so impressed with it that I bought premium after the first few glances. I love being able to track my meals and add pictures to them, seeing the nutrient and vitamin intake, create meals and recipies, scanning foods, tracking physical activity, steps and water intake. Interface is great and easy to use. There are a few small improvements that could be done

Really enjoying this app, it has everything I need for calorie tracking and intermittent fasting reminder when it starts and finishes, lovely feature for extra water drinking. Pro version is worth to get if you care about your micros and macros. I'm not sure why it keeps on disconnecting from samsung health and other apps, but that's a minor issue. Would recommend to a friend.

This Yazio - Calories Counter - & - Weight Loss - Helper - App'. By Yazio Is Exclusively Amazing For Counting Calories - & - Helping You To Loose Weight With Calories Counting - & - Intermittent Fasting!!., — Indeed!!.

So far it's the best calorie counter app I've ever used, and I'm quite experienced in this field) The ui is self explanatory, the product base is big enough, analysis feature helps to see the progress (or the reason why there's no progress). I've lost 18 kilos with this app and it helps me to maintain my results every day. Also it's a big advantage that I can sync it with my garmin device and it actually works much better than MFP.

I dowloaded this yesterday, and I love how easy it is to use and how many products you can choose from. I then found out about MyFitnessPal, and I had a quick compare. To be honest, I prefer Yazio because of the recepies that you dont have to search for, colours of the front page with each day visible ahead. In other words, I think you got the idea and made it better!
The app is great, but since the updates I’ve noticed that I no longer receive diamonds from the chests. The total diamond count doesn’t change.
i can now adjust my eating habits in a much healthier way, and i can now build muscle better and more efficiently!
great product. I 've used in the past to lose successful weight.

Easy to use and actually rather accurate I've got the pro version, but imo the free is also fine One thing I'd LOVE to some day be included is samsung Watch Compatibility. Other than that, it's so worth it

The app is completely amazing! It is a marvelous tool to help both beginners and serious athletes. Love the barcode scanner feauture which allows you to count calories of the products that you bought with ease! The free version includes all the required features. The premium version unlocks extras, like thousands of recipes for only 12$ a year which is totally worth it! I would really love to see a a zig-zag diet feature in the next version!

Very good app. I am using it for more than 1 year now and I am happy with it. It has a broad food receipts and products. When the product is not there, you have the option to add it yourself. I love the fact that you can track all the nutrients, vitamins intake and see where you need to improve plus many other settings. Yes, I recommend this app. I would like to suggest to the dev team to allow more detailed nutrition data to be added when a user contributes to the food db. E.g. zinc, potassium

I have tried many apps of that type in the past years, but this is definitely the best!! It is really easy to use, which saves time and makes it more probable that you keep track regularly. It also has some really nice and smooth UI. Recipes are simple, do not require fancy products and are easy to follow!! Insights are very helpful for learning! Plus, the premium subscription is not as expensive, compared to other similar apps!! I am very happy to be a premium member! It helps me a lot :)
great app, good features, nice UI. however it still double count the activities. what's the point of choosing my activities level if there is an activities tracker, and vise versa. please fix it.
this app is fabulous..im very happy with it so far. I love the fact I can change the serving sizes to suit because most cal apps serve 4 and upwards. the recipes are great as well
A really good app that I've used, I think it's easy to log into but you have to adjust your weight into their guidelines just letting you know!!

I’ve used YAZIO for 3 months and love the clean interface for tracking food, water, and activity. I’ve lost 15kg over 9 months in a healthy way—focusing on quality food and reduced sugar rather than restrictive diets. YAZIO helped me push past a plateau without needing a gym. Even my physician praised my progress during my yearly checkup! It’s a sustainable way to live healthier and stay on track. Highly recommended for anyone starting their wellness journey in 2026!
I've been using Yazio for over a month and it is an easy product to use with a great database of food and other ingredients. the app is user-friendly and easy to use.

I have been using this app for a year. The logging is easy and intuitive and macro tracking is great. I'd like to see all my stats summarised better -all in one screen and with the option of picking what I want to track. Various nutrition trends and selectable reporting would be useful to have but I love the fasting tracker and the food database. It is really easy to find your food or an equivalent, if you are logging homemade meals. Great app, will continue using it.

Great also for an average commitment, makes it very easy to keep track of your meals. The integration with Google Fit automatically adds exercise data to the diary. Absolutely good the recipes: just check for some interesting recipe and build the grocery list in-app, then buy the food you need and brainlessly prepared it, with assistance of the "cooking mode". could be worth to explore the premium.

I like the app, I've tried others, and it's fast and easy to use. My grief is that if I lose my tracking streak because I add my meals a day or two later, it doesn't reopen the streak despite all meals being 100% complete. I have a full meal log for several weeks now, but sometimes I miss adding the meal on the same day or two. Streaks should be saved and reopened if the info is completed retrospectively, that would be a lot more motivating. Otherwise, keep it going 👍

Great calorie tracker with strong analytics. Allows in-depth analysis of not only calories and macros, but other nutritional components. The food database is superior, and if something is not found, it is very easy to add. Recipes can also be added, and the app will calculate everything. All in all, it suits all my food tracking needs. Ability to add daily notes is a nice touch!

I really appreciate your hard work in this one, it's worth the try and has everything almost everything that would help you out throughout your journey. Only if you could, the fasting feature might need a little readjustment, if it is possible to add a manual fasting tracker, where you can choose your own time, would be better for those who have different fasting hours. So far, the app is great!

I was expecting something subpar compared to MyFitnessPal. But I was wrong. It delivered in spades.This is the best app for tracking calories (and not only) I ever came across. MFP may have a broader database, but let me tell you: YAZIO's is no slouch. I found the food I searched for in my local language, barcode scanner, very pleasant interface, integration with multiple apps (including Huawei Health). It's everything I hoped to find. I purchased the paid plan immediately. 0 regrets. Try it out

Loving this app so far. It makes it fun and easy to keep track of what I am eating. The recipes look and are delicious! Also loving the Mood tracker / Notes section. I do have a question though.. so my goal is to loose just a little bit of weight. The calories calculated are already the amount I can consume and still reach my goals? Or do I need to be under the number shown?

Purely from counting calories, I lost 55lbs in time for my wedding! Only thing I wish this app had was caffiene/coffee counting... you can log coffee in your meals, but theres no way to monitor how much caffeine your diet consists of inside the app, a feature I was rather fond of in Argus (another app similar to Yazio that has long since become an abandonware app). Nevertheless, Yazio is well worth your time if you're looking to get into counting your calories!

This app is pretty good, it has a very extensive list of foods you can eat to get the calories, even many local foods only found in my country (south africa). Very impressed overall, and it is also useful if you want to gain weight like I did, as it gives a recommended amount of food and exercise per day. I'd give it a 9/10, only improvement I would like is a nicer ui.

Integration with Google fit doesn't work. Anything you trying to do is worthless, Yazio just don't pull data about any activities you have in Google fit. I thought about buying premium, but now it makes no sense. If you are using this app to count incoming calories - it's pretty fine, but if you want something more, look for something else. Update: working fine, resolved with the help of support
Fantastic App and much easier to use with the AI support to load the calories from the photos.

Bought the pro this year. got a good price. so far I love it. the navigation can be a bit janky at times but once you figure out where you want to go it's fine. love the sync to my phones step counter. I think it's great value. entering in new foods can be time consuming at first but once you have your main ingredients entered it's so easy. tons of good looking recipes too if you need healthy ideas.

*edit: all of the bags are resolved, thank you and I love this app. There is a bag in the app, it doesn't count the calories I burn when walking, even when steps exceed 15k. Also, sometimes it just doesn't want to connect to the internet even though i have strong connection and i have to restart the app couple of time for it to properly work.

well this is the best. because #1 it is very simple to use which is very important key when apps is beening develop. #2 I like the colors they used to show u your carbs fat, protein, #3 no ad,' , #4 about 40-50% of the time u might find the food u eat on their lists. but I believe as time goes by their food list will be more. but I really like and I will for sure recommend it to others.

I'm still exploring this app, and so far it's amazing!!! for me it's a great tool to control not only the calories I consume, also the Nutritional value, which is crucial for a healthy weight loss, along side your fisical activity. instead of calculating myself, this app is doing it for you ! and you can visually see where you stand, and do the adjustment needed in order to achieve your goal easily. beside that, it's giving you so much more ideas. well done guy's! I love your app!
would like to have opportunity to export all of my food tracking with nutrition information for graphs in my projects. 15 days streak 🔥 going for 150 days streak.

99% of the food is in the database, and when I need to add new food is very easy. Only point of improvement would be integration with third party apps. I use samsung health and when exercises are integrated, the total amount of calories to eat is quite high. I usually eat the kcal in health app for the day - 500 kcal. Yazio seems to add already: basal + expected activity + external exercise - deficit estimate. Would be nice to set to 0 the expected activity if connected to third parties apps.

I've been using Yazio and have lost 25kg! The app's features are great, and I even purchased a Pro subscription. However, there's room for improvement. The water intake tracker doesn't sync with Samsung Health. In 2024, AI features like food recognition from photos and AI analytics for weight loss progress and overall health would be a fantastic addition. Despite these issues, Yazio has been crucial in my weight loss journey, and I’m not stopping here!

Absolutely love the app, the design is very attractive, everything is intuitive, and well explained. I strongly suggest to get the PRO version, as the recipes integrate perfectly well with the calorie counter. Improvement suggestion : I saved a lot of recipes, and I would like to have a way to classify them or sort them. For now the favourites recipes are just a "stack" of saved recipes.
I am a new user and very excited to use this app, as I have heard positive feedback from my friends
Nice app, makes counting calories easier and provides useful insights and nutrition tips 🙂
I fast 16:8; skip breakfast, and put number for snack in calorie distribution. but it doesn't appear on log
I was so pleasantly surprised with the new UI design!!! p.s. daily streak is at 206, it says it all (^з^)-☆
Easy to use and helped me a lot to track and reduce weight!
best food tracker, but no sleep tracker and activity tracking is not as good as other apps
incredible love it I just want to bulk up faster and get bigger maybe this yazio will help me get bigger I love it

This app is amazing. Even though I dont pay for the premium version, honestly you can do everything you need regarding weight goals. Really do recommend if you are starting a weight transformation journey. Whether its muscle growth, weight loss or gain or even just trying to maintain weight and being healthy, it sets a calorie goal specific to your type of body. 🤩🤩🤩

great app !. easy to use and helps you keep track of everything you eat. love how it lets you know what calories, protiene and carbs are left for the day. Plus you can input your excersise.It would be helpful if there were more weight options for the foods like 50,60,70 grams etc but you can customise just takes longer.

I was a little skeptical of the app at first, however it actually pleasantly surprised me. I am becoming genuinely keen to track my progress and I find the app's features easy to use and motivating, which is rare for me. Definitely changed the way I look at food, in a good way.

Really good app and helpful. I used for more than year a free version and it is good and if you stick with the program you can lose weight. I lost 22kg for year but i was also practicing. Now i payed for full version and then i realized how many great things, meals coaching this app had. Only one flaw and I recommend to try to correct it. When i making i list for groceries it would much easier to put all things in one list and if there are same products then just to add them together.

Great app. I subscribed now and used it for years. The only thing missing for me is to be able to set a timer which can remind me for example to eat next meal. So if im trying to eat every 2.5 h not to get hungry it would be nice to set on the app. Another improvment could be: for the app to automatically recognise fasting time. So when you load the meal it would only from than start fasting, so if you snack and break it, putting the food in the data will restart the fasting time automatically.

This app is great. I love the fact you can choose foods from specific places in order to get more accurate nutritional information and that it also has a water tracker to keep track of how much water you drink and to remind you to drink water so you aren't dehydrated throughout the day. It really is a great app, even while using the free version so I can only imagine how great its premium version is. Loved it.

Excellent apps. This is the one I've been searching for a long time. It includes the calories taken and considering the calories burned. Accurate or not, at least I have a guide on my aim to loss weight. Highly recommended apps to all!!!

I am impressed, it really works. When I do actually log in my food and eat according to the suggested calories, I do lose weight. Very user friendly. I got a 25euro deal for a year which is a great price. Highly recommend it, it does what promises.

now ill leave a post and review.. -ok so it been a little over a wk and i purchased the $20 for 1 yr..so far its great the recipes are really good which surprised the hell out of me because i thought they all suck but they dont they are really good..so far im using the yazio diet but with the paid version u can switch to keto, low suhar and more. i do think the free version should offer more like more recipes but it's ok the free app i would give 2 to 3 stars

I noticed that some foods like banana, or a tablespoon of olive oil etc. are being presented on the calories list more than once with the same weight (for example 100gr) but with different calories. How can I chose the right food and calories if I see it three or five different times with different calories? I'm confused... Still, its the best application ever.

Actually, I tried so many apps to calculate my daily calories, nutritions in order to gain Muscels. This app was really good. It's easy to use. Provide you with a chance to connect your fitness devices and it helps you to set goals such as stopping sugar or artificial sweets or coffee. I liked how easy to use it to track my daily calories

Really nice app to track calorie-intake and macronutrients! Especially if you are living in Austria or Germany, since the listed products and common meal are from this area. It doesn't hurt to speak German, though. It seems that not EVERYTHING is translated. Huge Plus for me: the recipes are amazing! :)

Love it. YAZIO also allows you to add your own recipes etc... I tend to take the same lunches to work and have variety of meals which I swap around so having them as lready saved is great. You can also add or subtract an individual item from your saved meal too which is really helpful. I did go Pro and haven't regretted it 😊

I positively LOVE Yazio!! I've been using it for over a year and have lost over 50lbs. It makes it SO easy to track calories, macros and exercise. It just amazes me! Plus recipies they offer are fantastic too! Plus there are way cool challenges as well! I'm not tech savvy at all and even I find it easy to use. I'm also not one to spend money on apps but you know what? I was so impressed with the free version of Yazio I bought the premium version and was even more blown away! LOVE YAZIO!!!!!!!!

Simple and yet thorough. User-friendly for the most part, although it takes a little getting used to. Customizable for recipes, foods and macros. It's worth paying for the premium straight up when you install to access all features, such as fasting, although not crucial. Love the colours and format, recipes and coaching tips!

Yazio is amazing to me. Not only does it help you lose weight and drink more water it also helps you to get so much healthier. I started using this app not knowing what macronutrients were. Not knowing how much fat or sugar, ect. to eat or why it matters. Yazio helped me to be more mindful about the things I put into my body and how that affects me. I would really recommend trying the app and maybe even getting the pro update for the great recipes. It really works for me and maybe for you too.

This is actually a pretty good calorie tracking app! Unfortunately for me, it has things that I also hate. After logging a meal or achieving a streak it plays an animation. There is no option to disable them so you have to sit there with your time being wasted while it plays. This may be fun for some! But it is not for me. There is also a meal tips thing that will pop up after logging every meal. You cannot disable it, though you can skip it. Extra work. I unsubscribed.

I have been using this app for about three weeks and have found it to be helpful. I like that you can include days where you are liable to eat more calories and I find the database to be smooth and easy to use. The only reason why I gave it 3 stars is because it doesn't track net carbs, only the total. I did pay for premium but I am having to go back and forth using yazio and another app that counts net carbs. If they could offer a net carb option that would be helpful.

I have had this app for about a year and a half. I have had the paid version for most of that. its very useful for tracking calories. its barcode scanner, recent section, and favorites section is very useful. a lot of the things I have every day are right there for me to click and add in the recent section. I like the notes aspect, where you can write down a note on the day youre tracking. theres usually a black friday deal that gets you the yearly pass for under 12$ which makes it very affordbl

Good, but few things need improvement. I wish there was a "free range" fasting option, because I dont always fast at the same times or same days, so being able to start and stop the fast without being in one of the presets would be an amazing improvement. otherwise, I wish I could put a picture of my meal next to each entry, and view the diary as a calendar. otherwise its a pretty good app.

Very good calorie tracker. I started using it shortly before the AI feature became a thing. I do like the AI tracker for when I'm like out but wouldn't rely on it too much. Just a few things I would like to see change. 1. Either make the diamonds useful or just remove them. it can be for different themes or something but right now it's just annoying as they're useless. 2. Add the done button to the search results and individual food items. it feels weird having to go back to say I'm don

This is definitely one of the most attractive nutrition tracking apps. I really love the fasting countdown. The only thing that keeps me from using it more often is that about 50% of all foods I scan don't have the correct nutrition information. I end up having to either input everything myself, or just change the serving size to get the calories close to the right amount.

Very good app, easy to use. A huge positive aspect is the lack of an avalanche of ads, like most apps do. One thing that could be improved, perhaps, is the accuracy of calories for the same types of foods, and measures (some use cups or ounces, when tablespoon would more appropriate, like for oats and cottage cheese). Overall, it's a nice app that can help with keeping the focus on the amount of food and water intaken, and activities done.

I haven't used this app for long but it seems to do the job really well. There are a *lot* of features locked behind the pro membership but just the free ones are a great help too. I might even consider getting the pro version some time in the future. Like others said though, many of the foods users have added are wrong which is why I wish there were more of them that were verified. Still a great app so far though.

Good for tracking calories, micros, weights and measurements. It works if you use it properly. The only reason it's not 5 stars is because if you don't log for a day or so, the notifications get rather passive aggressive about the ability to turn them off. That is a huge turn off for me. If I want to turn the notifications off, I will. Edit: the developer's response to my review informed me that I could turn the notifications off. I already knew this; hence why I mentioned it in the last line of my original review. Them telling me *how* to turn off the notifications wasn't necessary. I'm a 30 something. I've grown up with computers, etc.

Thoroughly enjoy this app and I have lost 12kg with it. My biggest peeve isnt with the app itself, but with people adding food to the database. Sodium is generally listed in mg, however the app allows us to add sodium in grams. This means a lot of food in the database is flagged as very high in salt. Easily resolved by changing the scientific notation to match those on nutritional panels. Another interesting feature I'd like to see would be some sort of 'calculate based on remaining calories'.

The app has generally everything I need, especially absolute great food database, but 1 thing really annoys me and lowers the score by 1 star. If i change my own recipe, it changes all logged instances of that recipe. I have to delete the recipe and create it again for that to not happen. Really sucks if i want to just change ingredients a bit. It would also be cool if we could set not portions but total weight for recipes, for things like soup or such. Otherwise, solid app.

It's nice but I can't set my goal weight because it's below the app's minimum. My actual weight is already there. I'm not tall and my build/proportions are not normal, please remove these limitations... The barcode reader is nice, but if it can't find a foreign something, it would be nice to search in a manually set country's database for that search. The summary charts are a bit hidden too. +syncing the calories into samsung health would be nice, i heard it can do that. Still, I recommend.

In general a good tracking app. But why change things that are good to things that demotivate you. The Meal Tips are just not very helpful. Why try to find just anything negative if the meal is already well balanced. If you try to correct based on the tips. It just finds something else where you can "improve" it does not help at all if your meals are already quite healthy. It just makes you feel bad for eating anything remotely "unhealthy"

​I have been using YAZIO for a long time, but a few things are becoming very annoying: ​Animations: There is no way to disable them. They are distracting and waste time. ​Meal Frequency: Please let us change the number of meals! I eat more than 3 times a day and hate having to log food into the wrong categories. ​Planning: We need a feature to plan meals in advance and mark them as 'eaten' later. ​AI Food Recognition: Please add a confirmation step or an option to manually adjust the weig

I've tried about 5 different calorie tracking apps, this one is the one I'm sticking with. The only issue I have is that compared to foodvisor, the AI tool for logging is not capable at all. Foodvisor will actually take the weight in consideration if I take a picture with my meal on a kitchen scale and will guess the proportions pretty well. it never worked in Yazio. Also, it can log a meal with just voice recording, with any detail you want. Yazio can't. almost perfect :( make AI better

I am really enjoying this app, the features (like the AI photo) are really helpful. Three suggestions I would like to make is a function to turn the calories into kj, have an option to turn off reoccuring "suggestions" (i dont like turkey, i dont like wholemeal bread... please stop telling me to eay them 😭), and the ability to edit the macros in the AI photo for more precise tracking.

Best app for calorie tracking hands down, but there are many problems, mainly the aggressive marketing of the subscription. I finally gave in and subscribed so the app would stop brothering me, only to find out even this isn't enough. if you're pro, but have auto renewal off, the app will constantly have an alert on the bottom menu, asking you to "Stay Pro". I am begging you Yazio, REMOVE THIS, I have subscribed for a whole year, I just don't want to forget about the subscription.

I have had the app for a year now and I did decide to renew. I like that it holds me accountable without being too annoying and meal ai search settings were a nice addition. Tho, Id like more for dietary needs. I like some of the meal tips except some don't apply to me. I have IBD and can't eat a lot of beans or whole grains and I'm allergic to red meat and dairy. The closest I could get my profile to reflect this is Pescatarian but a closer description would be Mediterranean or Low- FODMAPP.

The app is great and I enjoy using it. The past 7 weeks have been insightful with logging everything. The only thing I don't understand is how the daily diamond quests work. It says I earned a chest but it doesn't actually give me any chests to open nor do the diamonds get added to my total. A small thing for sure but irritating when it happens constantly.

It's a really useful app, already purchased the premium version as the AI camera is actually working well. One thing is not working (otherwise it would be 5 stars): the add buddies link brings you to the play store and the buddy is not added. Not just for me but for many of my friends as well. And it would be a great motivator to see your friends progress.. Please fix it, then its a 5star without questions!

Overall, I like the app but I wish the paid features included having the option to turn off the "I'm committed " prompts. There are just some days I can't track or don't feel like it. I don't need a slew of animations asking me to commit again and asking all these questions when I'm in a hurry. They should have animation instead when you log in some weight loss for motivation. Everything else works as it should. It works great with my Fitbit and I like the photo feature.

Really like this app. Used it, then stopped because I wasn't consistent. When I started another weight-loss journey, I tried not downloading it to avoid another subscription but I ended up missing the app and resubscribed. I think it's worth it's money (with the discount!).

I love the app and really enjoy using it. However, on Android, when scanning food with the AI camera, it doesn't show the whole picture—only a small sliver of it. I think it might be a software bug and would love to see it resolved. Everything else works perfectly!

It's a great platform but I'm not liking the animation and tips. I just need it to do what it envisaged EDIT I understand your response is to motivate but I'm not a kid and don't need the animation. it's a food tracker app... focus on that. Focus on foundation instead of dressing... like how your app has limited food options, gross recipes etc. And I am paying for this app btw.

A really good interactive app for sure. though there were 2 things bothering me, first was when it shows you a wheel to spin. I personally recommend if instead of showing the wheel to the user instantly, the user can have a choice whether or not to spin it and continue on with the beginning setup. Second, there seems to be a bug with the birth date. There were only two months and a day when you scroll to the 2011 option. if these could be fixed it would be much appreciated.

app works great, the AI bit needs some work as it's not fully accurate. The only issue I have is that it doesn't have enough apps for the automatic step counter function, I use GloryFit, which doesn't work with Yazio. Besides this, it's great!

Is it a feature to ask everytime the country I am in now? ( I have opened Yazio once on a short trip to Germany and scanned something... since that moment I have to confirm the country I am in right now). P.S. The same "feature" wasn't observed on iOS, just asked once, exactly at the right moment.

Really nice app but your fasting counter has a bug: If set to 12:10 or 14:10, it sets the fasting time to 10h instead of 12 or 14 respectively. And it also counts the time that has passed within the fasting window wrong - it is always at least 2:20h behind. This makes the fasting counter unuseable - any chance you folks could look into it, please?

I have been using the app for about a decade now. Have the Pro version. Overall pleased. I wish there were more of the nutritious keto options. And I absolutely dislike this new monster, especially with it turning into that fireball every morning when I track. In my oppinion it does the exact opposite of motivation - it steals seconds of my lifetime every day. Those "funny motivators" would be a reason to change app to a calmer one longterm.

am sorry i can't give a one star cause i get problem when I was payment... nothing complicated .. what is wrong here... . . . now everything is going well but need more work on Ai of the app ... in general everything is great

the app is one of the best in the market with one huge downside/improvement potential. it doesn't show if you have achieved your weekly or monthly but only daily target with yes or no. Therefore it's not possible to track your overall weekly or monthly calories which is a disadvantage. hope for that insight in the future as all the data is there. it just needs to be shown. usability and data base is superb

Yazio is simple to use and makes calories counting super simple with the barcode + search + AI camera combo. Here's my nitpicks for this app to be a perfect experience: - opening in multiple devices (e.g, my phone and tablet) breaks the streak system. - the "swipe left to delete record" functionality in the nutrition page is EXTREMELY ANNOYING because you can swipe by accident for a number of reasons ( I use the swipe on the border of the screen for the back button, for instance ) and no undo

I signed up for pro and have been using it for a week every day. The positive is that i can link it to my fitness app and tge ai photo option. I also like that when i add the food in manually it shows the estimated vitamins and minerals. However it doesnt add these in when the ai photo is used. If it can estimate the calories and the micros i dont understand why it can't add the vitamins and minerals. It would be great if that could be added in.

the best one ive tried so far. what pisses me off is that i got an offer for pro very early on but i had barely use the app to know if i would keep it and now that im sure i would like to pay for pro i have no offer. they should give it to you when you have time to be sure

please make it possible to disable the tipps and popups. users are asking for this for years now. generally, very goos tracking app. make it more simple without all the popups and fancy unnecessary stuff

I like it most of the times! The one thing that I don't like is that you can't set fasting times manually. I use 16:8 fasting all the time, but ocassionally I do a 36h fast. I have no way to set that so every time I do decide to fast 36h I need to break my streak or need to randomly add food that messes up my calculations.
The app is pretty good, but the new tips and the animations are super-annoying and is would be great to be able to turn them off.
please add easily digestible diet to filters of recipes and goals. also add option to add daily meal sections on top of existing 4 sections.

I have been using the app for several months. I think the Pro plan is actually worth it. I've been able to be consistent and lose weight. I was initially opposed to the addition of AI food tracking where you take a photo of your meal and the app calculates the calories and macros. But after having tried it out on meals that I cooked myself and knew the calories and macros for, it was actually surprisingly accurate and gives me confidence in being able to eat out. 4 stars for the new UI look.

Hello. I'm using yazio pro for couple of years, and it lovely, lot of options and diets, very useful. but honestly, last design update is awful and unreadable, it's kinda creepy. if you can — attach classic skin for choise please. if you wouldn't - I'll look for another app, but it's kinda sad for me.

I'm on a 536 day streak and within the top 1% of users. I lost 30 kg with this app. And I would love some more customisation options instead of new colours and an (admittedly cute) mascot. People have been asking for adding extra meals to a day for ages and as someone who likes to eat everything in moderation, I hate that the "food warnings" aren't optional. I don't care if my food is "processed" that doesn't instantly mean it's bad for you, so stop with the shaming please.

I really like the app I even liked the old design more then the current one I like having some color. :( and I wish instead of focusing on design like this they would focus on building some advanced functionality like adaptive calorie intake like macro factor. or the option to have higher calorie weekends and lower cal weeks. or the camera being smarter :), or if younconsume to Many or not enough kcal bring +- the next day, if update focus shifts in that direction I'd easily give it 5 stars

I wouldn't rate this app but i spent money on it and it was a waste... it's literally clicker because of useless and annoying questions that's wasting also my time... I want to quickly check how much callories banana shake gave me, but you didn't used our app for one week? here's some questions that'll waste minute of your life. edit: now it is possible to skip some of the time wasting questions, but not all of them.

I've really enjoyed using Yazio. It's helped me better understand the nutrition my body needs and I believe the food tracking has really helped me reach my goals, along with the recipe ideas within the app. The only reason I'm not giving a full 5 stars for this review is because there is NO monthly subscription option. You have to pay for the whole year, a full 60 euros all at once and that just isn't worth it for someone like myself, who will use Yazio for maybe 4-5 months in a year.

Overall I think it is great! My only complaint so far is if you live in a country that doesn't have as detailed nutrition labels it is impossible to add the item to be scanned by barcode. (Ie: Japan where fat and trans fat are not separate, and neither is carbs and sugars.)

I like it, but there's one thing I have not been able to find in any calorie counter period an app that takes into account a woman's cup size .. because they affect our weight. a woman with a large cup sizes not always overweight and it would really help if someone would try to invent this

I used this app gor about a week and it was pretty solid. Then I stopped but i ocasionally come back to it, and i find it a very anoying how every time i come back after a bit on not using it, i have to go through this whole charade of the fake roulette spin. Please add a skip button to this, its extremly annoying.
I have used a lot of tracking apps and this one is over all the one I keep coming back to. The only reason it doesn't get 5 stars is that it doesn't have a setting to track net cards vs. total carbs.

very good app, the only thing i would change is it would be good to have different calorie/macro goals depending on the day, for example, what I need to eat on a sunday rest day is different to a monday when I go to the gym and work, it would be nice to customise the days of the week instead of total goals

I definitely need one thing added: I've been trying to gain weight and I struggle hitting my daily calorie goals but I still get daily streak just for entering anything, I seriously need a streak that actually helps me stay motivated by following through on my goals because otherwise it gives me a false sense of accomplishment even though im not gaining any weight
I'm generally satisfied with it but recent version has a bug and every breakfast isn't shown properly.
popups are annoying after every meal you add (should be removed) but the ai image calorie calculator is pretty handy.

I'm setting up the app and I've got it on weight gain, I'm underweight and the questions are about eating too much or weight loss type of set rather than do you eat enough. like it's asking me if I eat more on the weekends, like I eat less on the weekends, is that an answer to the question. I know the typical question is weight loss but I've already got passed enough questions and put in my numbers. I just tried putting in a picture of of soda can and already want to cancel my membership
I like this app and use it quite often to track what I eat and how much I eat. However I didn't like the new branding and logo. It should have more diverse recipe options for vegans.

it's ok I guess... tbh the only thing that kept me from uninstall it was their mascot. I REALLY love it, but as for other things... many things are available only if you buy subscription, but that's ok. the only free fasting plan 16:8 which is kinda strange because it's not really beginner friendly. you can also add what you eat that day. overall that app isn't bad, but there's definitely better ones so... (I still absolutely ADORE their mascot it's sooooo cool)

loved it but a very important update we need, allow fasting to be flexible. it auto stop base on the selected fasting ratio, we should be able to manually start eating and end fasting because, let's face it, it is not realistic that we stick with 1pm - lunch or whatever.
Let me just start/stop fasting any time as opposed to forcing those presets
I would've given it more but am still trying it out. once it feels great, I'll right a good speech about it

I love the app. It is very beginner friendly and encouraging with loads of recipes to help. There are only two things, I'd improve: 1. It'd be great if the recipes could be filtered or searched by ingredients. For now you can only select a few pointers in what type of food you'd like a recipe for with a limited range of dietary options. 2. It'd be very helpful, if I could alter the given recipes for myself (e.g. the ingredients), so that I'd have an exact calorie count, and such.

Pretty cool, the database is big when it comes to brands and their products. Sometimes the descriptions need more information about what exactly is in a dish listed. I'd love a feature where you can privately add your own recipes! That would help so much and not having to type the ingredients every time. Also would prefer more ways to personalize the app, eg. not only put the goal of 30% protein, but be able to put the gramm amount. Also widgets would be helpful! Eg. a water intake widget.

So far I have only used the app for 5 days, I'm generally happy with it, although it could use some improvements. - Don't log drinks under snacks, but log them seperately. I drink coffee, tea all day, they're not snacks. - Add functionality to create custom meals (before logging them). The UI regarding logging custom meals is not intuitive at all. I would like to add some meals that I eat regularly, so I'd like to create them before hand. - Snapped food is not saved

I've been using this app for quite a few years and it is pretty decent. However, I was quite disappointed that the search option for one's own foods was removed; now one has to scroll endlessly until they find the food they are looking for. Furthermore, I would suggest adding a meal prep feature where one inputs the nutrition details for what one eats, but then the actual calories and stuff is only added to the daily counter once it has been eaten(a check off system).

YAZIO is one of the best calorie trackers I've tried, but, nevertheless, it has some issues. 1. It's impossible to customize meals (for example, to add second breakfast or to add a snack between lunch and dinner) 2. The Product name string is too short to fit more than about 33 symbols. Some of my meals' names are much longer. It is very inconvenient.

Great app to control what you eat and drink. Easy to use. I like the challenges that gives you - the no chocolate for 21 days is the one I am trying to accomplish. The only negative thing is that it doesn't give you the time to see what the app can do before you upgrade to pro.. It would be nice for new users to test the app before deciding for pro. It would also be better if the day turn green only when you reach your daily goal (not when it is less than the calories that you should eat..)

This app is so useful. I'm not sure how accurate the calory count is, but it does help me keep control over what and how much I eat without banning anything from my diet! The premium version offers you some recipes, but if you don't wish to pay for it, you can google their food recommendations. If this app helps me lose at least 3kgs until the end of this month, I will be purchasing the premium version! I have been eating less and been feeling more full thanks to this app.

I love the app, however, after 2 years of usage (on and off) I had to realise, that the macros in grams and the total calories they account for according to the app don't match. For some reason 1g of carbs/protein is not calculated as 4 calories, and neither is 1g of fat as 9 calories. I've been having a bit of leftover from carbs and fat lately, so I was happy that I could have a bit of snack, only to realise that the overall calorie count said I had already eaten more than I should have.

Useful app overall, although I'm missing a few features which could improve the experience even more. 1. Why can't we set our goal to maintaining or even increasing weight? 2. It would be nice to set a healthy range of calories, i.e 1800-2000 kcal would be green zone, above or below: red. 3. I'd love to see numbers... Average calories consumed per day in the last month, weight change in the last month... Considering not to renew my yearly subscription right now.

Very good app if you want to lose weight! Only downside is that you got to add everything you eat for breakfast, lunch, dinner and even snacks every single day. It gets a bit tedious after couple months. If you have a subscription like me, it will save what you have been eating to have a easier time adding it but it's still tedious.

I absolutely love this app!! The only reason I gave it 4 stars is because I really would love to see a Net carbs option and to upload recipes from the internet instead of having to type everything in every single time.

Simple and nice app.. Unable to synchronize my activities from google fit and noise fit smart watch.. If intermittent fasting timer has a n alarm, will be more helpful.. need to update my mail id as there is an typo error. tried several times but failed..

A truly well thought app for colorie tracking. What i miss to make it perfect volumetric analysis in AI scans for better understanding of the portions. Also i want to report a bug when i changed the colorie distribution it does not update my curent allocations

honestly really good app, but it wont let you do anything without plus. plus is cheap, but it would be nice to not have to spend money. either way its overall a good app, and its pretty accurate with its AI imaging estimates. awesome that it gives quick and easy recepies too! i would reccomend

Update: if I'm fasting, It often means that I will take a late breakfast. But the breakfast section when you're fasting is at zero. So if I eat, Let's say, 122 calories of fruit, The lunch snack and dinner sections are not updated to reflect that that part of the calories have already been consumed. This leads you to think you have more calories left to consume than you really have. Could it be more of a sliding scale?

Like the app. I would recommend some improvements: if my goal is set such that I wish to gain weight,  it's kind of pointless for the app to keep recommending a drink of water to fill my stomach if I'm filling hungry. It should bug me to go eat.   Likewise, it should encourage me to exceed my daily calorie plan. The way it works now, I get a green tick mark if I don't eat enough which is the exact opposite of what I'm doing.

Thanks for your work, it's a really nice app!Prefered it over others for its colorful interface and photos of recipes. However, too many options are blocked for non-pro users (I'd gladly watch some ads instead)and too many products from Germany are not recognized by barcode scanner. Therefore usage of the app on long distances would be too time-consuming for me.

Pretty decend application to reach your body goals especially when there are a lot of features which you can access without the pro version which includes some things like nutritions in your food, but it doesn't always annoy you to buy it which is pretty great. Other then that I have a feeling that when it calculates your calories it is prettunrealistic because I had the right data in the app and wanted to loose some weight but with that calorie goal per day I gained weight which wasn't great.
Great app. Easy use. However, I do wish I could add my own recipes for when I batch cook. It would be massively helpful.

All in all good food & calorie tracker, and i love that you can also indicate that your goal is to gain weight. however, the design really is not very "friendly" towards people who need to gain weight. i am currently battling my eating-disorder-induced underweight, and it is very difficult for me to see "x calories over" so prominently. same with the fasting menu, that is the last thing i need. it would be an amazing if you could turn off the "weight losing" aspects of the app somewhere.

It's a good app, tracks everything well and you can scan barcode to be the most accurate you can. My complaint is there's no skip option, I didn't open the app for a while because I've been maintaining the same meals and I already know the calories, I open the app again and I have to go through the whole introduction all over again. I wish there was a skip button

Good app, but it would be even better if support for kilojoules is added. There are millions of us living in the Southern Hemisphere, so support for kilojoules would go a long way towards catering for us as well. It is pain to have to calculate kilocalories each time we want to add a food item that isn't listed; there are a lot of foods being added with incorrect nutritional values as a result. Also, change your salt input to mg instead of g please as most people don't know how to convert to g.

Please, add an offline version!! Not even the premium edition contains an offline version. Also let us take a picture for the created (and saved) meals!! Lastly, the created meals and recipes should be able to be found in the recipes section!! Maybe sth like 'my recipes', and not only find them when you want to add a meal... However, it pretty accurate on macros counting, contains almost everything and easy to use and create meals for meal prep.

This app has been very helpful and has some great features, but I've found some difficulties. One: adding own meals / recipes is complicated, as others mentioned, and you cannot search your own entires (or I haven't found out how). The other is the message "the calories and the other data don't match" when I'm copying from the food's label. I'm trying to enter what's written there, what am I supposed to do?

UX - 5 stars, UI - 1 star. After latest update, UI is afwul and is disrupting my experience. I virtually don't want to open the app just not to see the UI. Old and new UIs is mixed making interface inconsistent. Maybe it is a bug (at least I believe that is).
too many pop ups when logging food. also, it should prioritize searching items that have been entered before instead of new ones I have never and will never log.

I stumbled upon this app recently and downloaded it. So far, it looks very promising, but it's just sad that they don't allow for a month, or even quarter of a year subscription. I mean, I should at least be sure of what I'm paying long term for by first paying short term and diving in fully if it works for me. I'll really appreciate if a month or fewer months subscription is considered.

It's very helpful, has barcodes of everything plus all the foods. And you can upload yours with macros and micros and save them. Also it's amazing because you can write down how you feel so you can connect it with your eating habits. Not to mention the diagrams of water, calories, nutrients, measurements and weight.. It would be nicer if we could save somehow the time of the meals. And also if we could send our detail data to other people somehow (for example our doctor or nutritioner).

I haven't subscribed (paid) to unlock all the features of the app hence the 4 stars. The app is easy to work, navigate around and understand. You can customize all your goals, create new workouts, I added chores with a little help from Google to find out how many cals they burned. Keeping track of your food, water AND exercise is available without subscribing, unlike some apps. The app has challenges such as No chocolate and No sugar days offering more than most apps WITHOUT paying. Love it

Helpful. You write down everything you eat and you see roughly how much calories you intake with every meal, every day, among other features. The "pro users are more likely to succeed" in my opinion is only because if you're pro, you don't have to deal with ads after every logged meal. And aside from that, every time I open the app nowadays it sometimes doesn't load at all. I often have to close and open the app several times so I can even use it. Otherwise it's great, I did lose some weight 👍

The app is great. I paid for the premium version because I found it easier to navigate than my fitness pal. Only 4 stars because I noticed a few things that could be done better. 1. Fasting- I'd prefer to be able to customise this fully as I like to do a 22 hour fast. 2. Recipes- It would be great if I could import recipes from the internet or pinterest. 3. I'd like to be able to log my coffee intake separately from water and meals. 4. Home excersize ideas would be great.
the app is amazing, It has a lot of Portuguese brands. I would suggest being able to change a recipe. this way I would not have to create a new one just because I reduced or added one ingredient 😕
it's a great app! easy to use so keeping up the habit wasn't hard. i wish the shop had more stuff or had a game that the gems could be used for.

It looks better now after a couple of updates. It keeps asking me to verify the country frequently which is annoying. My experience with calorie counters is 5 years and yazio is my daily tool since 2024.
pretty good and gives macros in the freezer version. however the ads to buy the subscription are relentless! and at least three screens
Apart from being glitch sometimes, its the best app for logging my kcal. A good value for the price. Big thanks to Yazio team for guiding me in my fitness jorney throughout the years🥰
I've used this app for such a long time and it is amazing and super useful, the only downside is the new interface which is less colorful and really uninviting... I wish the old one was back

I've been a pro user for years and much prefer the previous versions with less bells and whistles. Some actually useful stuff has been removed/hidden. These "motivating" features are not helpful at all, just annoying/distracting. I'd appreciate a basic version where i can disable animation, diamonds and all the stupid fluff. Make it optional. Also annoying that adding the same fruit for example but in a different language yields different macros which I absolutely cannot wrap my head around.

This is a pretty good app but a few updates could make it better for some reason this app adds calories for any steps you make which is a bit strange if you are trying to be in a calorie deficit and it adds on a extra few calories here and there. I have had to switch it off took a while and a conversation with a friend who also uses the app to work out why my calories were higher even though I hadn't exercised much this day. Also always prefer to weigh in Stone & lbs rather than working it out
There is an issue with updating the fasting time. The time for the previous day isn't accurate 😕

I'm using the pro version of the app, it's very good, but I wish you could customize the Coach feature by yourself. You can only choose recipes from the YAZIO database and "swap" them, while I would like to be able to choose my own recipes. Moreover, for some reason the Coach feature doesn't let you plan ahead your snacks (only breakfast, lunch, and dinner), which I would like to add too. I would appreciate it if YAZIO devs made this feature more flexible. Other than that, it's an amazing app!

I really like this app, it helped me lose 10kg and it's very easy to use, especially because you can just scan your products. But what's bothering me very much lately and causes for me not to use this app as much as I used to is the streak. It puts me under pressure and as soon as I feel that, I won't do it anymore. Could you please make the streak optional? I would really like to use this app more frequently again :(

After getting past all of the annoying questions, the app is ok. There are a lot of food options missing from their database, I don't see a way to add my own recipes, and every time I log food I get 3 annoying pop ups in a row, "here are tips, here is more info, you are on a streak" STFU! I just want to log my calories stop the pop ups! I have premium and I feel like I'm being constantly advertised to. Its annoying. let me opt out of those F-ing annoying pop-ups. My Fitness pal is 10x better.

Love the app. My only issue is logging of my food items. I try and I keep getting blocked with a message that reads... Oops something went wrong. Error Code: 412. Customer Support... what is this and how can I fix it or are you guys honing in on the problem with a fix soon??? Thank You. Will update my rating as I navigate the app extensively throughout usage.

I like the app. it's ai is generally accurate and I think the price is fair. however, there's an extremely annoying aspect that I cant believe has been overlooked. when you use the ai to snap a photo say of a whole sandwich... there's not an option to say you only ate half! I've figured out you can go in and manually adjust every single macro down to half by doing your own math, but why aren't there easily accessible portion sizes with the ai?!

add an option to turn off the meal tips!! i loved the summary after logging, which is gone. that being said, this app helped me gain 5lb, so it can be used for weight gain as well. i used to find it fun and easy to log my meals and it has a great database of US foods! it also syncs with my Google fit data & accounts for those calories, which is awesome! paid for the yearly subscription. edit: unsatisfied with dev response, cancelled my sub, STILL got a dot & notification for pro. ridiculous.

When you search food it doesn't have as many options as I would like, such as restaurants. It might have one calorie count for something and the one below it is completely different. It's hard to know what's right or wrong. You can add them in but I don't want to do that every time so I feel like I am guessing. I wish it would have things set as peice or amount. It reminds you frequently to log info and encourages you which I like, and has a lot of meal options. The meal calorie count is nice.

Needs better database. The overall experience with the App is fine. But, the database for food(s) that the scanner can recognize is lacking. Only about 50% of the foods scanned are recognized (especially if you're not in the US), so you have to type all of the information in manually. In the grand scheme of things, no, it's not a big deal. But, if the scanner feature was added for the User's convenience, it could definitely use some improvement.

Honestly this app would be really great if almost everything on it didnt have to be bought. I was trying to connect this app to my Samsung health app on my phone which is free and i had to unlock the "pro" icon on the app which i needed to pay for. Even to look at recipes or anything. Kinda annoying. I like the very specific details this app has allows you to pick and chooses specific diet plans. I would have enjoyed this more if it gave you more information as to why you should or shouldn't eat a high fat diet or how much protein we should intake. It was just vague settings that of course i could adjust myself. But what about those who dont know how much they should intake? Overall it needs some work. Its a like and dislike.

I like the app and the option to add custom foods and drinks, however the ads are getting insane. Every time I add something that I've eaten, and ad pops up after. I'm not talking after adding a meal, I'm talking after adding each individual food. I ate carrots- ad. I had a glass of milk- ad. It is making it difficult to use the app because of how often it pops up, instead of quickly logging my meal, I'm sitting there for 10 minutes trying to add my calories accurately

Truly disappointed with the newest update, makes everything complicated, food searches take forever to load, and the days are all wacky, some days won't come up, some are repeated. When looking at your diary you have to go into the meals to view whats in them whereas before they were simply displayed on the page. It was a terrible idea to change the app when it was just fine the way it was, and even more upset that I paid for a year of PRO to now have to deal with this poorly executed update.😒

I like the app but I wish it was easier to put in the exercises. For example I used the treadmill at a high incline and theres no option in the app for using a treamill or really any other gym equipment. I know you cann sort of put in your own workout calculations but even doing that is a little annoying. I do really like being able to put in everything i eat with the calories being pretty accurate!

Good app. Overall the interface is well designed, easy to navigate and easy on the eyes. Great that it can sync with other fitness trackers. A few complaints. They way the app deals with servings/units is confusing resulting in many erroneous food entries in their database. I am having to reference nutritional info on my food packaging to make sure the macro nutrients per serving are correct which takes a lot of time. It would be nice to be able to copy entries from previous days' food diary.

Okay. So I went pro thinking this app is great. I am aiming to lose just 30 more lbs to join the Navy. I have noticed a few things that are absolutely terrible. You cannot cancel your subscription and get a refund for your unused months. I'm now out $20 All of the food is measured by oz. And not by serving. Which can lead to you eating more than you should. I've noticed that the app tells you to drink water to fill your stomach and make you not hungry. This is very dangerous. You need to eat well rounded meals and not skip them. Also don't drink water just to fill your stomach. Too much water is dangerous. You should drink when thirsty. I have a Fitbit and the Fitbit app. They are supposed to work well together but I've noticed that they don't pair with each other all of the time. I'm having to input all the info into both apps. I've read the reviews and the way that customer service is responding it pretty unprofessional. They seem to only care about the money and not about their customers. I will be switching back to Lose it. The only good thing I've found is that their recipes are really tasty!

Downloaded for calorie counting, and while there many options for food input, there are no options for deleting foods or meals from what has been logged. So if I accidentally press the plus sign on an irrelevant food, or create a meal but add everything twice (which is easy to do when first navigating the app), it's stuck like that. Additionally, the vitamin and mineral content is very incorrect! (Super improtant), It only shows 3 each and vitamins at 100% will say 0mg/mcg. Please, fix this :(

As many have pointed out, serving sizes are all screwed up for a lot of foods in the database because it only allows you to record them in oz. Not end of the world but annoying. The main problem I have is that it doesn't remember all of the foods I've recorded already - only like the last 15 or so entries. So if I go to enter something that I last ate 3 days ago I'll have to look it up again in the database or scan the barcode. Feels like things takes too much time to do in this app..

The database is full of inaccurate entries, probably because adding foods is so cumbersome. I should be able to enter what is on the food label. Period. I shouldn't need a calculator to figure out per oz. values. Even if I go to the trouble, rounding errors often make the end result inaccurate. It doesn't matter what the app database needs. It's your job to take my real world experience and make it work with your code.

The food tracker is terrible, which is really annoying. A lot of the values are incorrect, a lot of foods are missing, and the unit options are ridiculously bad when trying to input new foods. It won't even measure by grams. I don't need the recipes, but I do need a good food log, and this ain't it. It's sad, because I paid for the pro.

Better than MFP but extremely difficult to navigate. I've been trying to figure out how to open up the barcode scanner for 2 hours within the app but can't figure out. The online help bot is worthless and describes a older version instead of the current version and the documentation lacks and outdated too...
