The only way I could figure it out was to use the widget... I don't want to type or speak everything I'm eating.

The inane and lengthy "welcome back" dialog chain is horrible, please stop. This app is, as an app, definitely better than "that other one." But it's limited: you can only sync from *one* brand of device; add another and it switches. (Google Fit, no... many devices don't sync fully or at all.) Polar support is broken enough that I went back to manual entry. Nor is it particularly innovative about food handling, and there's no website access: device or nothing. Still, much better than MFP.

To create a recipe you have to add an entry which is clunky, adding a custom food to a recipe doesn't work and won't save it. which wouldn't be a big deal if there weren't errors with the "verified" food items. Apparently Kraft mac&Cheese has 0 calories. That one is an extreme example but about 1/3 of the "verified" items have wrong info.

The program is fairly easy and intuitive, but the section boxes bounce around each time you open the program, the barcode scanner usually can't find a particular product(but often will if spelled out manually) and about 90% of the nutritional data and/or portion info that come up are just plain wrong according to what's on your food package. It's a work in progress. Added: also looked like it supported paleo/keto during the demo but I havn't been able to arrange it that way; so, it may or may not

I really dislike how all food is measured in grams. It makes adding custom food quite difficult, and in the end, I really regret spending money on this app. I really wanted to try out something new, but I am going to go back to using LoseIt! or Samsung Health. The recipes and challenges are nice, and the fasting element is unique to this app. However, if you are using this as a calorie counting app, it just doesn't work.

I wany to love this app but pleaseeeeee when creating a new food let me add the serving size in grams instead of just ounce/fluid oz!!! It's so much easier for me and most nutrition labels use grams per serving. Same struggle happens when scanning a barcode, let it scan the serving size and not a different one. I've spent so much time creating foods with the correct servings and having to google oz to grams in google just to make it accurate on the app.

So im testing out the app for about a week now. The app overall is okay but im not sure if its truly realistic. I logged my food for the day, which was fast food. Surprisingly they had the food in the app that i ate, however the calories were wrong so i had to manually add them. One thing that concerned me more tho is when i put in an activity. I was doing Auto Repair for 5 hours and apparently that burned almost 1400 cal. I dont think thats realistic. Otherwise id be muscle man.

I've paid for this app and have used it for about a week. I love it except for adding foods. I'm frustrated with having to type a general description in of each meal, and most of the time the meal that comes up is won't and I have to edit it. I've updated the app but cannot find a barcode scanner or any other way to add food. This makes it more tedious than it needs to be. I'll use it since I paid for it, but I would not buy it again

The app is good for losing weight and helped me to return in shape. However recently I had to switch to another one as clearly yazio is no longer improving. First the integrations. The compatibility with garmin just doesn't work. Secondly the the food modification has to go through their review that never happens. I entered my own food with small errors that are still there after one year. It's a shame because I love the interface simplicity but the lack of improvement is a no go for me.

All in all for the sole purpose of adequate tracking, it is a brilliant app. But seriously, I prefer it the old way. The new mascot and esp the meal tips are so annoying. It will always find something to moan about regardless of how balanced and healthy your meal was. Therefore it is not really helpful but i see it more as a danger to peeps that are at risk of eating disorders. Please make it a setting that can be turned on and off, so everyone can decide whether or not it's beneficial to them.

The App has some good features, i.e. data sharing with Fitbit👌! But it has two major gaps,🙄 on the food scanning: 1. It doesn't allow you, to store a scanned and revised dish, as a meal, for repeated usage 2. It's not prepared for Latin/Spanish eating out behavior - Tapas Sharing of dishes, since you can't scan a dish and divide it into equal portions (same for pizza sharing).🤞

It does what it needs to — adding and tracking food is quite convenient. However I'm very annoyed by the recently added non-skippable animations when I log my first meal for the day. Other popups like meal summaries and tips, or streaks are annoying too. I wish I could disable all of this and simply track my food. I don't need any of this gamification and motivatory stuff. What I'd like to see as well is an improvement to copying food to other days. Why isn't “today” marked by default?

It’s really good, simple to use for the most part. It really helps with my adhd. You track meals, snacks, calories, nutrition, AND daily water intake. You can create your own recipes which I enjoy I just wish it was simpler. EDIT: I KNOW....you have to manually type in the ingredient portions for custom recipes...the point is the way it’s designed is honestly stupid. All that extra work the user has to do when it could just automatically be a single serving measurement.

I've been using Yazio for 3+ years, and I've always loved it. I'm also a Premium user. But I'm writing a negative review cause I fully agree with everyone that the animations are way too much, specifically the yeti skiing. I'm personally a Yazio loyalist, so I'm not planning to switch for now, but please listen to the reviews that the animations are too much, take forever to skip over & I find myself spam tapping the screen to get over all the unnecessary pop ups. And no, they aren't motivating.

honestly, it's a fine app and I'm a paying customer but the design with the new mascot, albeit cute, is a bit annoying. it's too Duolingo-ified for me and after every log etc I have to click through multiple screens that don't interest me one bit. maybe give us an option to turn that off to have a clean version, I'm already paying and considering switching apps because of that, it's so annoying. that includes the meal tips, maybe make it a button where you can specifically ask for it instead

i like the app. it is easy to track food there and new ai tips are helpful. but i have major issues with the app 1. after i've tracked the food i meet like 3 extremely annoying unskipable screens with animation to "commit to my streak" which i have to go through every day to see how much calories i've tracked. 2. i can't use grams to indicate how much of the recepiece i've ate. it's inconvenient and causes inaccuracies in tracking

Could use 1 minor upgrade and I would be happy. Great app so far. AI tracking by a photo of food is quite accurate (I double checked by weighing and tracking seperately at the start). I would give 5 star if you made it possible to take a photo of my plate of food then another of the half eaten meal once I've eaten enough and subtract the remaining calories and nutrition information from my diary. Having the calories already tracked means I feel obligated to finish even if im full.

A lot has changed since my last review, when the app was constantly interrupted by popups for summaries, streaks, etc.. It improved but still needs work though. The streak feature still cannot be turned off, but it now only appears once a day for the first meal input. And the AI suggestions are not useful and intrusive, just another popup to click away. Please make stuff like that a setting. Otherwise, lot's of other useful feature, a good database and good overviews.

userName: l i x c l o v d
content: Easy to use, very intuitive and overall one of the greatest tracking apps! But, I have to admit that lots of features have unnecessarily become for pro users. I can't even plan ahead what I'm going to eat the next day because "it's a pro feature" now. A lot of people have very busy schedules and find the day before the only right time to log their food and keep their mind organized, like me.

Please fix the app guys. I'm probably one of your first users and I've never faced any problems but now the app does not sync with my Huawei smart watch and I've never had that problem before. I have used a Fitbit and Garmin in the past and never had this problem. Already tried the Huawei Health guide and all other troubleshooting methods. Please fix the app

The app is okay. Has a lot of features locked behind a paywall subscription. I would consider paying for it once though. The new UI is going to make me switch to another app though. Its a lot less clean and the new mascot creeps me out. edit: also looking at other recent reviews, it doesnt seem like the dev team takes criticism seriously.

Good app, has a good range of tracking options and you can find 97% of the foods... however the PRO version is the biggest waste of money. The ai cam is very off and the tips you get after tracking a meal are all the same : add a salad and eat less quantities no matter how little you add. it's only worth if you get the lowest offer (i got it for 97% off)

Ive only been using the app for a couple days now. I like it so far but my only issue is that its constantly trying to get me to do the paid version. Everything I do I get the pop up and I cant immediately exit or say no. I have to let it do its thing and hit continue to be able to exit it and move on. Id rather have ads.

A couple of necessary upgrades I would recommend to keep me using this app should be making it more customizable. Having the ability to place snacks at different times if the day instead of just in one spot. Having the ability to turn off accounting for activity calories. Being able to import recipes into the recipe creator. Bring able to scan labels into the food creator. If these options are already available I apologize, but Have not noticed this. The app is good, these would make it better.

I want to subscribe but can't afford to pay the yearly amount all in one go. When on the subscription page it makes it look like you can pay monthly at £2.99 a month but when I go to pay it wants to take the full amount for the year. Please consider making this a monthly subscription. Or if this is an option and I'm just doing something wrong, please advise me on how to do this

it's an amazing app to track calories and macros. I've used it for years to track my food weekly. however, with the last update, it's not allowed anymore to track food in advance and, for people like me that we meal prep and know what we are going to eat everyday, it has become a tedious task to have to daily input the same meals. paid version allows this...hopefully you'll amend this as it used to be great!

The reason I use this app is because it's popular in my country so it's much easier to log more niche meals because chances are somebody already registered it. Overall simple to use. The AI photo function is helpful when feeling lazy but inaccurate. The reason for my lower rating is that I absolutely hate the way this app jumped in the trend of copying Duolingo. The only reason I stick around is because of the above mentioned perk. It's very annoying and off putting. I wish it was optional.

Its only usable when you buy pro because they basically pressure you into doing so each time you try to log literally anything. Apart from that its a pretty good tracker in my experience, but i would personally like it if the app layout was more customizable in the sense of maybe having themes or different colours.

Good app but unbearable ads. Why not give us a decent plan for 1 or 3 months?In this case, it's free, but annoying as hell. I'd suggest an HONEST price per month and I'm willing to bet you'll have plenty of clients. Another problem with it are The food nutritional facts. The approved ones have mono saturated and poly saturated fats 1000x the actual amounts. Reported it, they don't care. Also, some stupid gamification features that are front and center, but add zero.

I love this app, but man they really annoy you with ads. It's literally every time you add food, not only do you click through an ad but you also click through their "promotional offer" to go ad-free. We get it, you want to annoy people enough to start paying for it.

Baisc functions are great, I even like the meal insights. On the other hand I find the forced gamification super annoying. I don't want to click "I'm committed" and other silly motivational popups. Personally don't like the streak functionality either. Just make these things optional and it's easily a 5 ⭐ app (:

all works well except one thing. it won't allow extended fasting tracking.. I should be able to decide for myself how long I want to fast for. it's becoming very undeniable that a multi day extended fast is good for your health. So why not allow users of your app to track this? it's really frustrating that I can't simply select when I want to start and finish my fast on the app... please sort this..

Great app, but the way it forces the user to endure an endless useless and boring unskippable test is crazy. Had to close and open the app three times. After the second time I decided to delete it, but accidentally opened it and it finally let me in.

I have been using this app for many years and rated it with 5 stars. Now, I feel like I'm not enjoying it as much as I used to. I don't have enough characters to write a full review, so I will only share the remarks that made me less fond of this app: - the "processed food" label feels too judgy - too many clicks within the app (especially now with those challenges/tracks and encouraging text pop-ups) - the rebranding is way too childish (the font, diamonds collecting feature, the mascot/logo)

edit: where's the data export? isn't the app required to have one per EU regulation? the app is easy to use. the only thing that really really bothers me are the animations. every time i see an animation I can't skip I just quit the app and hope that when I reopen it's not there anymore. I think they should introduce a mode without animations. I just wanna open the app, log the food, and move on.

I was looking for a casual calorie calculator. I was bombarded with endless irrelevant questions. Then I could find a way to skip a premium subscription. In the end I gave up before I was done with registration. Didn't give up on my goals, but I'm going to work on them with another app, that doesn't need to know all my dreams and experiences. I'm sure those things are relevant for some people. But if you need a casual experience, this one is not for you.

Please add more customization options. 1. Auto tracking (or pop-up confirmation) for fasting when adding foods 2. just because I'm fasting doesn't mean I want to skip meals. breakfast is just the first meal I eat no matter the time 3. diamonds for tracking during my fasting times sucks (especially if you do add auto tracking) Otherwise I really like the app, adding my own foods/meals etc is pretty easy. sometimes navigating can be a hassle and I can't remember how to get to certain screens

Earlier, that was a good app to write food. But now it is too heavy, slow loadings, unnecessary AI tips and animations. I just want to write my meals, nothing more. Add settings, pls, to disable unnecessary features.

Personalizing fast is impossible, so if I forgot to edit it yesterday now I can't tell the app that I've been fasting since last night as I can only edit today's time. Also, even though I'm committed to a plan, it's not always possible to stick to it, so I want to be able to tell the app that today I've been fasting for 12 hrs rather than 16 and still want the app to track fasting phases properly. Such lack of flexibility really gets on my nerves.

its ok, barcode scanner is great however many of the macros scanned dont match the details listed on the food. creating meals etc isnt straight forward and you seem to bounce around alot trying to get it to work. the idea is great but it needs some work

It's too many pushes to get you to pay for the app. It finds something wrong with EVERY food entry. Some suggestions are ok buttttt EVERY entry without being prompted is unnecessary. But overall an ok app but nothing I'd highly recommend.

Great App before the upgrade to AI with this annoying Yeti creature and the "meals tips" after every entry. There needs to be an option to turn off these meal tips as it gives me absolutely no benefits akd just makes me uninstall the App and get another one.
Disappointing. Some great features but this is not easy app for users with food allergies. Was recommended to choose veggie diet although my only allergy is egg.

app is awesome but like my Fitness app sayd i burnt 42 calories on my rowing machine. i couldn't type in 5.03min or 0.52 km so i had to do 5 min and 0.5km with then sayd i burnt 32 calories 10 calories different then what my workout app sayd. still really good app do would suggest 😊

Since the UI change it is a pain to use. Harder to find meals in the search, not the most recent ones are on the top. These should be there in decreasing order. Also meal tips pop up after anything added to a meal. Very frustrating and waste of time. Yazio is getting worse, definitely doesn't worth the price anymore. I just got to skipping days to record because it is everything but not quick to do.
i really like Yazio, it actually helps keeping me on track, BUT the AI-suggestions are not useful and often sound condescending. as a paying customer I would love to be able to turn them off!

The Dietary restrictions section is too limited. I have IBS and just can't have certain foods. Not even an option to select "other" here. One of my main struggles is finding the time and motivation to eat healthy within this diet (I have to make a lot of food from scratch to avoid my triggers). This could be accommodated in the app that would be amazing.

I like this app. But I gave 3 stars because there's one thing that really bothers me. And it makes using this app a pain. If I forget a few days to count my calories and if I cone back even if it was less than a week, it makes me answer a LOT of questions and I have to redo a bunch of stuff I had done before and then only I can input what I ate. I hope they discontinue this feature as this makes coming back to use the app difficult over time. And it makes you think you will never succeed.
it's nice. I don't see a way to do custom fasting. always forced to end it on the app as per assigned schedule. when changing the schedule, it resets.
AI tracking, from my experience, can UNDERESTIMATE the calories by a lot. For those who want to lose weight, that is terrible.
It overall good. However, the AI photo function should also include how much grams is the food on the plate for added accuracy. Similar to Macrofactor
The database is well-organized. However, the application is experiencing some bugs, and managing recipes within it can be cumbersome.
Has a bug in the AI camera mode where only half of the image is processed, but on the vertical line e.g. only a thing vertical image is seen by the AI, on the left
I have the paid version and the features and functionality can be improved. For the members who are on subscription model, the app developers should be constantly adding new elements.

Hello Yazio Team, It's a great app that helped me lose weight. However, could you please consider bringing back the barcode scanner feature in the free version, even if it’s available by watching an ad? It was a handy tool for tracking food and calories, and many users would appreciate having it back. Thank you for your great app and support! 🙏

the app's good and does its job well, lots of foods available, my only remark are the notifications because they don't get sent regularly, a full day can go by without having received a reminder to log in the foods or sometimes the notifications for every meal come all together at a random time in the day. I'd like to have separate reminders for Breakfast snacks lunch and dinner not all together. Thanks, my device is an Android onelus 9 pro

Update: I don't care if it's supposed to be motivational, make it optional, I don't want to wait for them every time. It's bad UX. Why do I have to get through 12 screens and 128 animations just to track my food? It's getting more and more ridiculous, please stop, or at least make it an option to turn off all these "fancy", unnecessary stuff. I don't want to spend 3 minutes just for one food item. I pay to use this app already, why do I have to pay with my time too?

it's good, but most of the items I scan are not available, I have to either enter it manually, or find it's information and record it down. another thing is when I have to record a new item it requires all the nutrition info about it which I don't always have (such as potassium, magnesium, etc) or it does not accept it.

Used to be 5 star. But don't like the updates. Earlier with one click on the mainscreen I could track food with the barcode now it's 3 clicks away. The animations are cute but a waste of time. I don't need gamification, just a minimalist quick interface. Not sure if I will extend my subscription.

I bought the year subscription because I really liked the app, but I don't like the new changes at all. Please make the 'meal tips' and the Yeti 'fireball' popups optional. I want to turn them off but there's no option to. I chose your app precisely because it didn't have stuff like this :(

1. I have to say it's amongst the best tracking apps I know so far and I've tested a few. Good database, good interface, good customization options (goals, macros). 2. BUT: I totally get it that some people need motivation and tipps and therefore animations help them getting there. But a few amongst us are professionals, like athletes, coaches or similar and we do not at all like a ton of animations just stealing time - PLEASE provide an option in settings to make them disappear...!

okay so I love this App. I've been using it for around 2 years & I love the way it's structured and simple to use. Recently I lost my streak despite having a streak freeze due to a bug. After getting told where to write a report I got a pretty quick response which is great. Yet sadly losing this streak probably makes me switch Apps. I do not have anything against the App I love it, but the AI use makes me pretty sad. If there was a way to turn it off completly I would keep it.

Is okay with premium if you get a good discount. don't pay full price people. the intervall fasting function really needs some work. it is driving me crazy. Just let me set fasting start and end myself and not force me into some arbitrary schedule you have cooked up. also loosing your streak and progress when changing fasting cycles is not good.

I've been using the app for years because how easy it is to find and track meals. it used to be a five star app. Recent changes have made it less so. The AI feature was fun to try once, now it means one more click to get through to the five star features since I cannot remove it myself. Then to top it off I now have to watch an animation after I've logged my meal. I want to be in and out of the app and keep going with my day. Not like this...

Was about the give up after few min of endless questions and motivation messages. recording food is not that easy i.e. intuitive. a lot of clicks. and then the neverending buy pro offers. the code scannner works well though. De-install from my side, i find Gemini easier to use and faster.

Have been using the App for over 500 days and while it has helped me achieve great goals the Animations have just become hugely annoying. Having to watch the Monster Turn into flames every day (or whatever it is supposed to be, I dont really care) feels like a waste of time. Rather than giving useful features like exporting my own data the developers seem to have been focused on making the App worse.

i've been using Yazio since forever. but the recent updates and the fact that I always have to click continue is just the opposite of user-friendly. and sorry, but the new logo and the animation are scary and weird, not suitable for a meal tracking app. sorry
I tought I downloaded calorie tracker app, not survey simulator. It took 9 minutes to set it up without even reading questions properly

every time I eat and log, this thing criticizes and tells me I could do better. there is not even one time after a meal it's ever like "good job! you did better than yesterday!" it wants me to be perfect in a place where I don't have access to the things it is asking for (like whole grain bread does not exist where I live) it's just constantly "of you do this, your meal would be healthier" and even when I make the sub it says to make, "if you do this additional thing," ridiculously critical!

Overall the idea is great. Lots of interesting functions and you can find most ingredients / foods and even fast food / convenience food that is saved within yazio. But there are a few drawbacks. syncing with a fitness app didn't work for me, even with support help. there are way too many adds for a good damn food tracking app and these adds are really lagging my phone out. Overall it is fine but I'd say there are better options

100 questions just when you start the app , I just wanted track my calorie intake and they are even asking if I am single and do I have kids, literally, and you have pay premium for the ai snap feature ( price is reasonable). This more of a data collection app than a calorie tracker.

App could be great, enjoyed it for the first few days but then I realised I was spending more time skipping adds, most of which are constantly prompting you to upgrade with a limited time deal, which just appears again the next day anyway. Got to the point where I was instantly closing the app to skip the add without even checking my stats and it felt more like a chore than helpful.
Wish it had exercises tracking but I general ok, hard to edit food and see what I ate that day a bit confusing

Easier to use than MyFitnessPal but the AI feature barely works. When you try to correct a meal, half the time it just freaks out and just says "cant contact server" then logs whatever it originally suggested with no option to edit. It really only works for simple things like sliced apple. I paid for this app, so it'd be great if this could be updated.

I used this app and I still use it, but my advice for improving it is to reduce the number of ads and don't focus solely on profit. Make it a good subscription for users to develop it, rather than making everything By subscription I hope you will reduce the number of ads and make Food tracking with ia free.

the app is good however I find the animations and maybe three screens I need to click through telling me I am on a streak or I'm doing great really annoying. they waste my time and if I need to go and add another food item I need to wait for a retarded animation to finish

Overall good app but the user experience has a lot of space for improvement. Creating and managing recipes is particularly painful (when tracking a meal if I go to recipes there's no immediate option to create a new one, if I want to create a similar recipe there's no option to duplicate an existing one and I have to add everything from scratch, etc) I'd gladly update the review of some QoL improvements are made
please add an option to stop the meal tips, I paid for the Pro version so that I can log my food as fast as possible, and that just makes it extra long tapping it every time. The rest is good

Favorite foods section doesnt work. I created a meal and it is not there. (that section specifically mentions that created items will appear there) I would also like if the quick add option would allow us to input sugar,saturated fat and salt.
the app is nice. doesn't work well with health connect. If you're using android I'd look for something else.

this is my favorite calorie tracking app, its simple and really easy to use, with a big pool of food selection. But the app is also filled with ads unless you pay for the subscription, everytime you log something in you have to sit throught ads, it becomes annoying.
it's decent but could be better since product from thailand when scan barcode shows unable mostly

3 stars. for an aplication that is supposed to make you more efficient you spend to much time inside it. if you want to find how many kallories you have in one dinner you waste at least 20 minutes. you could use that time time to eat slower and actually enjoy the food

it used to be better... now you have to pay for taking a picture of your food and automatically adding it. the logo and animations kinda make it look like a little kid's game. it would be five stars if it was how it used to be.

My previous review was 4 star, but I downgraded it because of recent changes. It used to be so convenient to open app, log meal, close app. Now you have to filter through multiple screens to get to where you can log meals... and I'm a Premium Member! Sorry Yazio, but I pay for convenience, and this app no longer qualifies. So long, and thanks for all the fish. I'm out of here.

I am super disappointed with the most recent UI changes. I took a break from tracking for a few months and switched from Apple to Samsung during that time. It was such a pain setting up my new account with the forced data entry to start. Then I immediately re-subscribed to premium thinking the worst was over. I wish I didn't pay for a year in advance because the streak pop-up, goal setting, etc you have to click through is SO annoying. Please give the option to toggle those off for quick entries

good app for a good price but not perfect. I hate the fasting portion and having no way to edit history or customize my hrs and meals. example, I would like to chose a 14:10 but I don't want to skip a meal. I find I can still have 3 separate meals and would like to track it as such but the app will not allow that and requires you to skip a meal. I have been using this app for 3 months and just tried the fasting portion and immediately became disappointed. I want to track my fasting my way.

Mediocre - I paid for pro to connect my Garmin watch and use the fasting option. Fasting tracking is super disappointing. The app stops the timer even when you aren't done fasting. It's also not flexible with days you don't want to track, like weekends. there doesn't seem to be a way to edit fasting entries. Adding to my review - needs a vacation mode option.

Initially enjoyed the polish of this, but I regret paying for Pro. It's too slow and too convoluted to track meals, which is the whole point of the app. We need a way to disable the useless (and incorrect) AI food tips after each meal logged, at least. Between that, the lag when searching for foods, and the various submenus for foods/meals/recipes, it's too much. Even saving common foods as a meal and selecting that every morning feels slower than other apps because of the tracker layout.

It's a good app, the only one I've seen that lets you select your country to give you food options from the stores here. But meal planning is impossible. The moment you exit out of one of the menus, it pelts you with stats, ads, premium subscription sales and "You've earned a streak freeze". It takes 6 taps to exit one menu. It's become so annoying, it's better to just close the app and reopen it.

When multiple recipes are added to the shopping list make all the ingredients multiply as needed & condense into one list. Like, for a week of meals it shouldn't be seperate lists for each recipe, it should be just one shopping list. Maybe that's really hard to do? Also, some recipes say they take 10 minutes but then the instructions say things like "roast for 30 minutes". Why you do this?!?!? Calories are super inaccurate.

The Update ruined it. Previously, the app was a breeze to use, priced well, and very useful. Now the UI is uglier and everything takes more time to input. For example, you can no longer see the brand of foods you search for unless you click into each item that shows up. Finding a particular food item in the database now requires numerous more clicks and a lot more time. It's genuinely a pain to use now and I can't recommend it.

If I had realized how central fasting was to this app I wouldn't have bothered subscribing, it seems like a pathway directly to disordered eating. I should have done my due diligence while researching counting and activity calendar apps. The vaunted barcode scanner is missing tons of things. Be prepared to spend a lot of time paying to do data entry for yazio, at least as far as the first times you eat certain things. Theres no simple way to add home cooked foods. Or I haven't found it anyway

Pretty disappointed. I used to use this as a food tracker and was pretty happy with the app so I decided to download it on my new phone about a year and a half later and now it makes me fill out a bug long questionnaire and then I try to sign up at the end and it won't let me make an account. Keeps saying error 409 and doesn't even tell me what's wrong. 😒 I just wanted a food tracker and it was really useful for that before.

I've been up and down on this app, but most recently what did you do to the fasting tracker?! It used to show a timer so you can see how long you're currently fasting, but now it's a countdown until your feeding window. So if I extend my fasting time I still can't see how long I've been fasting at a glance because it just resets the countdown. Please please please change it back, I'm regretting paying for this app and have no intentions to renew now.

Certain changes to the app should not have been made. The price jump is insane to where it jumped from $19.00 a year to to now $85.00 year. Before the price was reasonable. Not for sure for why this price increase. Makes me second guess the app now. Don't get me wrong, the app is still good and useful, but now i can't sync my polar flow app which was helpful.

I kinda wish I hadn't been sucked into buying the "new and shiny" subscription. The app is really slow to search foods, the pictures don't match the items and everything is measured in ounces. It's a real pain when you're trying to adjust for portion size. My morning shake is a great example. I use 1 teaspoon of peanut butter, but the app offers 1 spread, 1 cup or 1 oz! I waste time trying to convert a teaspoon into ounces which makes the food diary process frustrating and I won't keep it up.

I really liked Yazio previously but since a year or so (since I used it last), there have been some changes that are not to the good of the app. I.e., the meal tips after you've entered your breakfast/lunch/etc are nice at first, but then become repetitive and you can't turn them off. Also after entering your intake, you get an animation that you're now on day X, with additional, longer animations on days 10, 30, etc. You can't skip these animations and it just gets annoying.

the app is ok, but unless you have the premium version of the app, it's practically unusable. Adds keep popping up when trying to log food, or using the app in any way. Really annoying. Also - the app advertises an AI function to take photos of food to calculate calorie number, but this option is only available for premium users. Overall - not a very good experience.

I loved this app, it was the best! I hadn't used it for a while – I had a premium for year, but then I couldn't pay because of my region. Recently I decided to return to at least a free version and it's almost impossible to use! after each tap in the app, you get the ad that you can't close for several seconds:( upd: got a paid version and now can't get rid of all the gamification things. would be great if I could turn off this duolingo in my yazio

I honestly hate this app. I paid for it, so I still use it but it sucks. Adding meals is way more complicated than it should be. I hate those suggestions it gives me after entering in a meal. All the suggestions are telling me to replace food with veggies or salad. I can't replace everything with salad. I lost 3 kg with it, but that is only because I'm tracking my calories. I would like to understand how exactly the AI feature works because I don't think the estimates are correct.

bought it and now regret. Too much noise and distractions, unwanted tips and gamification. No option to turn it off or customise. I wanted a tracker app, and not another green owl. Every time getting lost in the UI, even if I simply want to input meal records. AI camera works relatively well. UPD: -1 star for the useless automatic reply.

This used to be the best calorie tracker app, but since the last update... mweh... The new UI is much worse than the previous and the animated figure is very annoying. But not as much as the useless recommendations which cannot be switched off - even with the Pro subscription. Almost nothing is recognised properly by the algorithm so it is as useless as the AI. Get rid of the stupid figure, revert to the previous version and work much much more on the AI if you are so keen to integrate it.

The database is very lacking for food items in Japan. Have to manually input most things, so it makes everything hard work. Infuriating that you are forced to input everything per 100g or 100ml when things are often listed by portion weight here. Also annoying there are mandatory nutritional info sections you have to input. Sometimes they aren't given on Japanese products. Feel like I've wasted my money.

It is a calorie counter and it works well in that respect, hence the extra star. But... it is the worst piece of annoyware I have ever come across. Requires unreasonably lot of clicks to perform the simplest tasks. After succeeding to finally register a meal, you need to click your way through 3 to 5 completely meaningless screens. Registering every piece of my intake is a fuss I hate. It's not gamification that helps me. The UX should be as fluid as possible, not as cumbersome as possible.

i upgraded to paid mainly to stop the interruptions and just use the app. But the streak feature popups and meal tips kept showing up constantly, just as annoying as ads. i checked with support and was told these can't be turned off, which is disappointing. on a paid plan, features like these should be optional. i just want to log my meals without being pushed toward things I didn't ask for.

I used the premium app as trial and was considering paying for it after my trial period, but I won't. When I manually enter ingredients and quantities for a meal during my meal prepping, ingredients regularly disappear and I have to re-enter them, sometimes 2 or 3 times for a meal. I thought it might be a one time bug but it keeps re-happening. It makes tracking very difficult and tedious, especially since I don't always remember the exact weight for each ingredient.

Constant pop-ups, animations and "good advice" you never asked for, are exasperating! Relentless in hindering your day with no way to switch it off. Gamifying a food tracking app is a terrible idea! The app is alright, very basic, clunky UI is counterintuitive. Database is OK, sometimes patchy and inaccurate, and macros are off. The ai tool is just meh. You can have a pal but you can't share anything with them, like meals or recipes. what's the point? + for a functional widget and syncing

Good app and I’m still on Pro, but after every meal you have to click through annoying app messages (unhelpful tips, streak screens, summaries). They slow everything down and can’t be turned off. Over time this makes the app unpleasant to use. From 5 to 2 stars.

Ai tracking is ok. Im currently using foodvisor which has a better ai tracking and has a quick add feature which is very handy. i paid 24 dollars for 1 year subscription for this app and i wish it was cheaper 😅 . oh well lesson learned.

1) I'm in the UK, why are all my foods in German or French? and yes it says I'm in the UK. 2) you need to add text or voice based AI to this. The camera isn't enough - I want to be able to dictate what I ate and the AI make estimates. 3) I agree the branding is weird. but I get what you are going for - Duolingo for calorie tracking

This app is good for tracking calories, but the animations are tedious and meal suggestions are very spammy after everything is logged - I don't care! I want to be able to disable these. I also find quite a few barcodes are missing compared to other apps I have tried.

Using Premium for 1 year. 1y ago this app was one of the, if not the best app for calorie counting. Simple and fast. Over the past year this app has changed so much. They added so much gamification, unnecessary animations, etc. You cant turn it off. Even with premium. The animations take more time than tracking the calories on itself. Its extremely annoying. We pay premium to avoid adds, save time and then get spamed with these things. I will no longer be using the app, changing to another App.

so many questions. What are the diamonds for? why can't I edit my fasting duration for the previous day? if I exceed calories on 1 day why does it not adjust my intake for the rest of the week to ensure I'm under my weekly limit? I paid for the year. if these things are fixed I'd give 5 stars as I love the gamification element but these a few QOL updates that would make this app one of the best

Basic functionalities like viewing created recipes and custom foods are hidden behind menus. Very non-intuitive UI. Also the incessant push to subscribe to the pro version after adding any food is beyond frustrating. Overall using this app was a very frustrating experience. Uninstalling and won't be using this app again. Also the app doesn't accurately import calorie information from the paired health app. The free version is absolutely useless.

Warning: latest update removes analysis section. Recommend disabling auto updates. IMO was a 5 star app until they added mascots, AI, game-ification, animations etc. With analysis removed I will finally have to stop my sub and find a new app, a pity because this one was so good before.

Will be cancelling my pro because of the useless updates. No one uses the AI tracker because it's not accurate, and the monster branding is an extremely weird choice. Please include an option to turn off animations. They take up too much time and add nothing. Not interested in the "motivational" messaging. I just want to track my macros. Adults are using the app, not children.
Latest update has removed the analysis section, personally I like being able to see how I've progressed from one weigh in to another on a chart. Also recipes are very lacklustre.

good calory tracking app, but sadly ads have become obnoxious, forcing you to open and close the app store or browser for any weird app. using it way less now, and trying to shortcut my daily info as much as possible.

What I don't like is that you cannot just describe the food like you would on Macrofactor or ChatGpt. it's inconvenient when you couldn't get your phone out to scan by camera. I hope this function gets added. The food available on the list is very inadequate and imprecise. I tried to type grapes but I got grapefruit. A very inconvenient app. I regret subscribing. Also I don't understand how the fast tracking is useful. Why is it not capable of pin time in my notifications like Duolingo does.

YAZIO used to be a great, fast food-tracking app. With the recent updates, many new features feel drastically annoying and slow down logging, especially the constant food tips and bloated interactions. I’m seriously considering canceling my subscription. Please remove most of the unnecessary food-related fluff. The AI food capture is the only new feature that’s actually useful.

unless you pay for the app you cannot use the photo feature or see any of your macros which was kinda the whole point. you'll also be asked to buy the pro version after you manually log every food. if you dont want to pay 25$ (or more) just stick with my fitness pal.

Good app for tracking, but the gamification elements and affirmations are extremely annoying and largely unnecessary. Please remove the chests and streak stuff, or at least give me the option to. Edit: they made it worse, now I'm forced to watch an animation of a yeti that can't be skipped??? Going back to loseit when my subscription ends.

been using for 6 months now and the one thing you cannot do is save Ai scanner results into favourites also same with barcode scanner can't save to favourites if anybody as worked this out can you make a note and speak to technical as they have no idea either was using food advisor before not a problem just crazy expensive

Literally unusable without paying for a subscription. Every time you log a new meal ingredient you are sent through a loop of unskippable offer screens and ads. Actually made me stop tracking because I couldnt handle clicking through so many screens every time.

It was amazing. The best calorie tracker I have used. I paid the premium immediately, and kept waiting to see how much it improved. It didn't, it went backwards. I can hardly ever get a streak freeze because the chest feature (that is extremely unnecessary and rather useless) never works. Also AI does not need to be everywhere, things aren't automatically better just because you slap some half baked AI into it, it makes things WORSE! Seriously considering canceling my subscription at this rate.

app is good, i am using a pro version, but i will not renew, the logo is giving me bad vibes. and i don't like that i have to do so many clicks in order to get to my diary. and i don't care about the rewards system eather. you eather do it or you don't. i get why it's important for the development of the app, i just don't care for it if it has addictive properties.

Their database and AI photo are the best across all apps. UX is the worst. It has all these childish engagement patterns which make it very annoying to use. Everything takes several clicks/screens and it gives you advise you did not ask for. Using my old app in parallel until their AI photo catches up.
unable to see the daily/weekly/monthly reports under the profile. I hope they did not remove it because it was the reason why I loved this app. I am pro level.user.
the app is good overall but making options and features paid that were free before, is actually ridiculous and made me quit this app after using it for more than two years.
immediately had issues with adding custom meals where none of them would be saved for later reuse and we're pretty clunky to use considering their main "easy to use" promotion.
I appreciate the extensive food list for my language. But the app itself keeps reminding you to purchase the subscription every time you click "return" on your phone, which is very annoying.

I used to like this tracker — I even rated it 5 stars before. But now it has become really irritating. I have a paid subscription, and I constantly have to skip all the challenges, tips, and animations. I just want to log my food in the tracker. Please make these features optional so they can be turned off.
I set my calories goal, but the Summary page still shows and calculates as my previous goal, which is wrong and crucial to me.

good app but I don't like the new branding. the weird monster theme makes it look like it's branded towards kids. it looks childish and takes away from the core point of the app which is to track calories , I feel it should be clean and professional looking. the font looks weird now and the fact the app is now called 'AI calorie tracker' shows the direction they may be going. not sure if I'll be keeping my subscription if it carries on this trajectory

too many and highly manipulative ads. using all the tricks to get you to subscribe. from "yes chains" to fake "win a discount" games. you have 2 tries and always only get the lowest discount first and with your second try the highest. just so you feel lucky and impulsively sign up. obviously directly commiting for a full year, because 3 months prices are unfairly high in comparision. do charge people money for your app/services, but dont try to trick them.
I remember this app being excellent. After I long time I re-downloaded and the adds shoved down my throat were 10 times more grueling than a diet. this alone is a reason to change to another app

Please stop with the yeti and the AI tips. The core functionality of this app is great, but all of the fluff you've added around it is infuriating to navigate. I've cancelled my subscription and will look into alternatives.

inaccurate nutritional info. even when I take photo of clear label of the nutritional info on product pack, AI guesses the nutritional info based on the type of food which isn't accurate at all. would like it tweaked to use the information and calculate if it's available instead of guessing.

I joined pro and will definitely use a different app when it expires. database is good, but - ai food tracking doesn't include nutrient details. useless for anything more than calorie counting - meal planning tips pop up constantly, and you can't turn them off - takes 3 clicks to move a food between meals, rather than allowing drag/drop - lands you into food adding menu on open, you must click done to see the day overview - it emails me in my locations language rather than the one I set in-app

the tips and the ai calorie estimator isn't terrible but for an app that is 10 years old, the food database is rough. Large number of items listed in oz/grams is fine for portions like vegetables. Entering food from restaurant and listed options as oz/grams. Selecting sandwiches from a list and none having serving of 1 sandwich is a little frustrating.

Very heavy on gamification, asks way too many questions with limited answers, goes right on asking for subscription without even having used the app, then offers a 1hr limited time discount hoping to trigger FOMO. - I just wanted to try out the app for nutrition tracking, I don't want to be quizzed, I don't want subscriptions being forced down my throat all whilst not even having had the ability to use the app. Have the devs even bothered using the app themselves?

It was really useful but ultimately i cancelled my subscription after a 100 day streak because there is just too much annoying stuff. too many gamified things that i cant skip. long animations that are killing my phone when i only want to quickly track something small. EDIT: one update later, it got worse!! now i am constantly asked to turn on notifications that i intentionally disabled, and suggested meal improvements. all i wanted is simple calorie tracking :/

never got to use the app... 20mn into answering mindless unstoppable questions I have up... dude I have a life, make these questions skippable! just wanted to track food, not even sure if it does that in the end! will try a less annoying one!
Since days it is not working anymore. The main page shows only a plank white screen. Only works after phone restart. Then happens again. Not usable.

The app in terms of UX is good but it's lacking some basic functionality that at the end makes you not use it anymore. For example impossible to take a picture of nutritional infos (even with AI the values are off) to prefill the data. We have to enter manually everything which is very cumbersome.

Nagyon szerettem ezt az appot, de egyre több funkciót tesznek át fizetőssé... És most, hogy már egy nappal előre nem jegyezhetem fel a kajámat, a meal prepping teljesen lehetetlen az ingyenes verzióban 💀 I used to love this app, but more and more features are being put behind a paywall.... The newest one is calorie tracking for the next day, which means you can't prep your meal the day before. This renders the app unusable for people without a subscription. Why be so greedy?
Waste 15 minutes of your time only to let you know that there are no trial or any useful free options. It's even more depreciating to users than full screen ads!
The recipe sharing is not working. Very inconvenient that we cannot share our home made foods with our spouse. it worked last summer but not now :( The design is worse after every update.

I liked the app for a long time but the animations for meal tips and the streak feature are taking too long. I dont even want those features. Please let me disable them in the settings. Im a paying customer. Sometimes i dont even want to open the app because i know the animations will driving me crazy. And please stop with those diamonds and that shop. Nobody needs that in a Health-App.

I used to love this app until they started implementing ai, was not needed at all, it was already a great app with many options and detail. at least this encouraged me to get a free calorie tracker with no ai, which works beautifully.

Search results are completely random, it's ridiculous. enshitification and dark pattern at its best they want to trick you in getting pro on every screen, just hitting continue on a randomly appearing popup and it already asks you how to pay. as everybody can add new food to the catalogue it's overflowing with the same things. tried to add a missing product by barcode, recognised a wrong code, putting manually throws an 415 error. found no way to balance meal calories.

I have lost 25 kg with the help of Yazio, but....This app is actually making me crash out. They keep adding a paywall to things that were completely free before. It's really pissing me off because now i can't plan my meals for the next day. Additionally, they keep trying to shove their new Ai features down my throat. The app used to be alright before, I didn't even mind the ads. If they keep up with the unnecessary updates, I might actually find a different app instead.

With every update, the process of tracking meals gets more tedious with annoying "motivating" animations and extra steps. The main reason for using Yazio for me compared to other trackers is their excellent database. With the new Streak animation, it is faster to close and restart the app than to wait for the animation to finish. The only thing these motivated me to do was cancel premium. edit: there's nothing in the link from the response about animations. also I don't mean ads as I had premium

It used to be simple, plain, an excellent tracker, now it takes more time to track, ui is very bad especially that yeti thing that comes up every time without being able to disable is ridiculous. Listen to your users/customers otherwise people like me will not renew the subscription. LISTEN TO YOUR BASE.

Been using this app for years now, absolutely a fan. I just wish one could turn off the AI search as an option because it just adds an extra step to my logging as I never choose AI photo. And bring back the verified options button please Update: the coach tips after every single log are very annoying, please add the option to turn them off Please also add an option to save your own recipes in the cloud, as losing an entire library just because of a phone change is incredibly frustrating

Solid App mostly used because or the compatability with Garmin. Recipe system should be overhauled, but all together would still be a 4/5 if not for these HORRENDOUSLY ANNOYING popups after each logged item (streaks, widget suggestions, quests). They ruin the whole usability and upset with each use. They can not be turned off. Just awful.

I've been using the app more than 5 years and It was one of the best calorie tracking app up untill few years ago. UI got bloated with unnecessary additional screens and effects. Logo changed from a beautiful apple to a monster, UI color changed from beautiful dark blue to pitch black and UI design now looks like duolingo. Also removed challenges for healthy eating habits. Added AI, I guess the user data being used to train AI models too. So, I cannot recommend this app anymore.

The experience is nice as a paid user, its easy to track calories and scanning items works well. However it's laborious to edit or share recipes and it would also be nice to have an option to share meals per WhatsApp or with the buddies feature. New update is horrendous and there is bo option to change the color scheme.

After every food I track there comes a food recommendation I can't get rid off. It is annoying and not useful. Where can I turn it off? Why do I pay for pro to get this content shoved? I would rather have a widget for quick tracking with the AI tool. It takes too many clicks to use the main functionalities for me. And: An API documentation would be so great. How else should I get my own data out? Or at least a csv export? Otherwise we all have to ask through mail for GDPR.

pro user here. too much wrong information to track vitamins and minerals. they depend on other people's input, which is not always correct. tracking vitamins, protein, good fat (not seed oil) and minerals are much more important than total calories. AI never gets the right input.

Tried to track food, got interrogated instead..Yazio has a clean interface and the snapshot feature seemed useful but it’s locked behind premium, of course. Too many unnecessary personal questions are required upfront (work hours, children, etc.), I'm just a young dude, and you can’t skip them. Also forces a weight goal even if you don’t have one. Feels like it was made for old aunties with specific health goals, but for a teen just trying to track food easily? It’s more hassle than help.

there are a billion and one adverts for pro every 5 minutes — i get you need to make money but its excessive. i haven't been using the app very long, so take this next part with a grain of salt, but to me, it seems that they are taking their normal features and making them pro. yesterday, I could track for future days fine, this morning it told me i need to pay. I have a limited space so I'll cut it short, this app is amazing – only if you pay. if you aren't willing to do this, don't bother

Tracking now three times as long as at the beginning of the year. Animation and "AI" Feature making the App not usable for me. Sadly, up to this point it was great. I will cancel my subscription if the bothersome new feature will not be optional.

was nice, it's very bad atm. this suposed to be a calorie track app, now they put all kind of stupid things in to make it harder to use and to lose a lot of time (who need a streak updaye daily, or to ask for AI foto of your plate everytime you wanna add something, or to get a meal suggestion everytime). we want a simple and clean app, not this (like it was 1 year ago). if nothing changes I will cancel subscription and move to another app.

Hello! I do like yazio and do want to continue using it. An only thing I don't really appreciate is an AI meal tips,and I haven't found how to turn them off. Is it possible? Or will it be added in the future?

I have rewrite my review, but my stance stays on. We dont need animations, and the food checker is not motivating in my opinion, that part should be optional to use. And as of you giving out unsolicited advice, you should listen to your costumers and make a app thats for daily life, not a game like app.

1 week in with mixed reviews - I was very happy until it stopped syncing with Fitbit and continually asks to reauthenticate to no avail. The app for some reason doesn't have write capabilities to Health Connect either so I'm stuck without food tracking in any of my other apps now AND I've already paid for PRO...

I've been using this app for a while and I have a 360 day streak and have lost 32kg or 64lb but these new updates to the overall UI look terrible in my opinion now it feels like a glorified kids app with this new look and 8 honestly absolutely hate it and it's making me consider switching apps cause the old look was easy to navigate and was honestly way better looking than a lot of other trackers but now it's like a kids game where's you have to add numbers I honestly wish go back to normal
too much gamification. we don't need Duolingo, we need to track decent food and to be able to edit it easily

Latest updates have worsened my user experience. Due to unprovoked rebranding, apart from having a subjectively ugly new icon and tolerable art style, for some undeserved by me reason, every first of-the-day meal entry is followed by an unskippable and boringly long cut scene with the said ugly mascot. I'm seriously considering deactivating my subscription and changing the tracking app. Too bad they don't listen to their core users.

It works but DO NOTICE BEFORE BUYING: There is NO WAY to remove "meal tips&suggestions" Pop-Up -screens after logging what you eat. They seem to start after a couple of weeks even if you are a paid customer. Even customer support states it like this. Sadly I paid before knowing.

Way too many popups and things to click thru. I just want to track my food, but, subjectively, after every click there is some sort of message congratulating me or showing stats or something. please just let me enter food and display the nutrients. over time it took more and more clicks to track my food, to the point where I uninstalled out of frustration. maybe I will check it out again in the future but as it is now the app is unusable to me. sad, cause I was a premium member for many years.

forced options of meals can't micro put in details of meals. If ur goal is to lose fat every calorie counts in a calorie counting. you will be off 300 calorie under or over. plus the ai scan never correct only a shortcut so don't pay for app.

Decent app but a long way to go. It really lacks food data and the servings are off the majority of the time. It takes so much extra time to adjust the food to the proper serving and have the calories matches. Also the biggest flaw it only takes some data from Garmin, doesn't sync over weight body fat etc...

Used this app since 2020 and while the data is good, i like the qr scanner, the quick overview for cal & macros I'm barely using it (and i have a membership) because of all the extra clicks and flashy interruptions, stuff i need to confirm to commit or whatever when i just want to enter some data really fast amd go on with my life

Beta testing on users who pay for the app should be optional, not forced. The app keeps changing and making things more challenging to use and less enjoyable as a whole. I've used Yazio for many months but have decided to go elsewhere.
cute app for logging food, the ads are TERRIBLE. They pop up every time you log something in.
You're asking me to rate the app before I've even been able to use it. I'm still in the middle of an unnecessary 50-step introduction quiz
Answering questions for 10mins about my habits and still not in the app. And I'm pretty sure the next thing it'll ask me is how I want to pay.
Would be 5 stars but WAY to much ads and pressure to buy the pro version. Like WAY TO MUCH
way too many ads, also why does the pro ad pop up every 5 seconds? really annoying and it makes it unusable

I love the design and functionality! However, the app currently supports only the legacy Google Fit sync, not the newer Google Health Connect. Since Google Fit is scheduled for deprecation in 2026, this limitation prevents syncing activity data from sources like Oura — making it impossible to have an accurate, activity-adjusted calorie goal.
used to love the app, but after the changes it's just ads and AI slop

lose it premium is half off for a year. process asks 80 questions. Just let people use your damn app. To the reply: I understand the point of onnboarding but there still needs to be an option that says "Let's stick to the basics" where you enter in age, height, weight and target weight you had so many questions I was just trying to answer the bottom one and skipping ahead to the point i almost signed up for premium. then you had me spin a wheel twice then we were finally at the login page.

I used this app for years and loved it. But i recently had to uninstall it because of a crazy surge in ads that made it unusable. Also, the recent pop ups that make it impossible to quickly and smoothly use the app. And for some reason, now if you miss one or two days it makes you go through a lengthy "welcome back" process that is actually just a convoluted membership ad. Im very disappointed, wont be coming back.

I thought it might be good, based on reviews saying the interface is better than myfitnesspal, and I liked the cheaper subscription cost. However, the food database is disappointing. I have lost count of how many items I have had to create before I could log them. Most foods have to be added by ounce or gram, so if you make a recipe that calls for one can or box of something, you have to know how many oz that item is. They should have a per can/package option. Try Nutritionix.

Terrible application. Most of the time the current day does not even load, making it so you cannot track your calories. I purchased the pro version. The developers do not seem to care that their app has a substantial flaw as they will not issue me a refund. On top of that issue, when it does work, you have to pick a single unit to measure in. Either grams or ounces. I measure food in grams, but milk? Can't do one or the other. Also lacks a data base and can't enter food when the app doesn't load.

So far it's extremely frustrating. If you make an err, It's either impossible or a long frustrating process to correct. I accidentally entered my weight wrong. It won't let me change it. So, my total weight loss is inaccurate every day. Many times you can't enter the correct amount of food you've eaten. Like if you only ate a Tbsp of peanut butter, it only lets you choose oz. I signed up for Pro, it's too frustrating to continue to use, so just out my money.

Worthless. Do not get this app! All this is, is a fancy calorie counter and goal timer with some recipes. You have to pay to use any potentially useful features. I paid not knowing everything that was offered. Once I opened the paid version I was extremely disappointed in what was actually offered. My Fitness Pal offers almost all of the same stuff in their free version so WHY would anyone pay 20 bucks for 1 or 2 extra (pointless) features?! Also, they will NOT give you a refund once you pay.

Not as good as MyFitnessPal. MFP has way more food integrated in their system. For example, food delivery services such as Factor 75 is non-existence. MFPl has a smarter search engine. Whereas in Yazio I'll type in "Chick-fil-A grilled chicken sandwich" and I'm going through 25 listed items before I get to it. These things may seem small, however, things like this are a major key factor when it comes to committing to documenting foods. Also Yazio has no free feature 👎. Would not recommend

The app offers very little customisation and very little in return for the expense. The customer service is a joke. Upon trying to speak with them, the only response I received was "well you can cancel the membership". Don't waste your time or money on this lack luster product, they will take your money and offer little in return.

This app is unusable without paying for PRO. Every useful feature is locked behind a paywall. I understand the need to make money but I would be more willing to pay for extra features if the base product were useful. I would also have to create custom entries for all of my foods, the ones in the app are all incorrect. Edit: Stupid response, didn't read. The app is unusable without paying. The free version should make you want to pay! That's the problem, not food!

If you respect your time, your sanity, and want to actually track you calories with ease and keep track of your fasting period. DO NOT GET THIS APP. People rave about how there aren't any intrusive ads, but they fail to mention nearly everything is locked until you spend money. I apologize but a fasting tracker is literally just a timer that's connected to a graph and you want me to pay you for that? Spend some time browsing some older apps that do these functions, but haven't doesn't want $$$.

I thought I would like this app. I don't normally do reviews on these ever. But after using it for 16 days I have discovered many foods are inaccurate with nutrition. It's pretty disappointing. I guess I should have had better expectations for the price. I paid for a year. I would have paid for my fitness pal again had I known this. I tried a new one because of the cost. As a bariatric patient, I need accuracy and consistency. Now I'm out of the money for nothing.

don't buy the "upgraded" version. all it does is give you some crappy "personalized" plans and some crappy recipes that you can just find online for free. waste of $20. when you buy it make sure to unsubscribe or it will charge you $20 yearly forever. it only have oz and fl oz as serving sizes, so you can't enter things as scoop, cup, package, piece, etc. it's very frustrating trying to guess which one is the size you're eating. this is like the loseit app but crappier.

Start up process was overwrought, and so weight-focused I had to just lie to it try to hammer through the million irrelevant questions. I don't track my weight, I just need to log calories. Had to sit with the profile editor to figure out how to make it get out of its own way. Even then, the diary interface is cluttered and frustrating. How the heck is this even a recommended app?! The only thing going for it is that it didn't force me to make an account.

I purchased pro version by mistake and Yazio personnel refused to reimburse the subscription despite it had been few days and that I explained I could not afford the subscription, which is charged for the full year and is very expensive. To me a good enterprise's revenue is the result of positive impact to customers, not from mistaken purchases. That's why I want to review with one star hoping they can reconsider their perspective.

Just a cheap Lose It! knockoff. Except this one only lets you count calories for free with a smaller, limited food library. Everything is pushing you to pay for a membership, but if you're going to do that, just buy a Lose It! membership because it is a lot better quality. I have been using them for years, just tried this for a promotion. This just feels gimicky and the UI is really outdated and cheap. Don't bother with this. It can't do as much as Lose It! And doesn't look as nice either.

Not good. I went and did the whole intro and then it asked to "sign in" with Google or email but you cannot get any further then that. App doesn't work. Going back to my fitness pal...UNINSTALL..I don't care for an app that has "technical issues" before you can even sign up for it.

I tried the app during the free trial. After a couple of days I found it frustrating and difficult to follow. Unpleased with the app I tried to cancel the subscription before they could charge me for it. Turns out they still did and customer support has been nothing but useless. Neither Google play nor Yazio could help (even though they can). I wouldn't recommend the app due to the interface and of course its customer "support". You can find the same and more in free apps. DON'T WASTE YOUR MONEY!

After installation I'm prompted to log in with an email account (Google) or as none of the above. Regardless of which I choose, I then select "Start Now". At that point I only get a blank white screen with options for Back or Next, neither of which do anything other than present me with the same white screen. Uninstalled and reinstalled and the issue is still there. It's hard to imagine so many great reviews for a product that won't even launch successfully.

It truly serves no purpose. Used it for 2 to 3 days and quit. Just focused on my progress by recording myself (not posting/not a blogger) and lost 15 lbs in 7 days from 162lbs. It's overpriced specially if you take into account that you're not able to log in your workouts or meals correctly. Timer is hard to reset. Set your phone alarm, log your meals in a diary, monitor your progress every week (not every day). It will keep you focused. Wish I could give 0 stars, fasting works, app does not.

it was better in the past, now it's just annoying to use as a free member. every time I track one of my meals and hit confirm, it just gives me unnecessary meal tips that I can't turn off. okay, I can live with that. but after that comes an unskippable ad for their premium benefits, and after seeing it FOR THE 15TH TIME I am just so tired of it. make it at least skippable. it takes me so long to track my meals at the end of the day, because I need to constantly close the app to avoid the ads.

With every update it becomes a little bit worse. It rebranded as an AI company but their AI adds exactly zero value. The whole "AI powered tips" thing looks like a kid trying to tell you how to track your calories. It's just one more thing I need to close or ignore between me and what I actually want to do. Also, it doesn't work very well at all. The AI camera makes things up and it's just wildly inaccurate. I want my old tracker back. I canceled my subscription. I'm not paying for this.

Subscribed by accident and tried to get a refund a minute after I paid but unsuccessful. Now stuck with the app, I am even more disappointed. Not that many recipes, many recipes are obvious like scrambled eggs 🙄 and not any filtering options. For example, I tried choosing high protein but every option was really high in saturated fats and many included protein powder (which I don't have). I can't choose high protein and vegetarian at the same Waste of money! Not sure why this app rates well.

Insane amount of pop ups. This app used to be good a few years ago. There were too many pop ups even then, but God, as time went on they just got more intrusive, more annoying, and so deterring. I already find logging calories a pain, adding a million pop ups just makes it so so frustrating. I've even paid for premium for a year. Didn't get rid of the pop ups. I remember now why I uninstalled this app. Let me turn them off and I'll consider redownloading. I can't handle it in its current state.

Stay away from this app. Used to be the best tracker for food, good price for PRO as well, now it's the best at annoying you and making it not want to use it anymore because you spend more time closing popups and seeing stuff you don't want to see. If I wasn't sure, I would say they are just trying to kill the app intentionally... Went from subbed all the time to regretting paying for this.. thing..

Tbh the app isn't really bad but what is really grating is the gamification and unwanted advice they're desperately trying to force onto you. I just want to log my food, I don't need advice, and I really don't want any diamonds, chests, streak logging, insistent messages about losing my streak and stupid cartoon interruptions. If they made these features optional, I would give it 5*, but they're persistently ignoring user requests to remove this rubbish, or make it optional. This isn't a game!

Million questions before you can get even the app open for the first time (the personalization, yeah). Can't use the AI camera logging unless you take the paid pro subscription so actually you can't log any food without paying. You can't even try if the app works well enough without paying. Not really a good approach. 👎🏼

even paying the app, after log in something there are still annoying splash screens happening, unnecessary gimmicky Animations of this unnecessary monster turning in to fireballs or giving unnecessary tips and so on, just let me log in my data without the unnecessary gimmicky stuff! i feel like its a cheap phone videogame! i don't need that, make the app clean and free from this garbage or at least let people choose to turn all this garbage off! once I found an alternative i will leave this app

I loved the app, it helped me lose weight. I barely use it now because the streak system and the gamifacation. I hate it sooo much. I wish I could turn this awful feature off but can't. Don't reinstall the app (even if it'suggested), because you will lose your streak. I lost mine, and there’s no way to recover. After a month synchronisation with health application just stopped, even though all tickboxes are active. The AI sucks. I took a photo of the nutritional information and it kept adding random numbers like it didn't know it was looking at the nutritional values. Why do you have an AI feature if it can't recognise the very thing it is tracking? Otherwise cheap and very extensive list to track. would have been a 5/5 if you didn't make the AI a selling point.

In the beginning this app was so fantastic we bought it for both of us, but now we feel forced to switch. 1) The meal tips, animations, recommiting, it's like being forced to watch ads which I paid to not have. Should be an option to turn off. 2) The Yeti, we don't celebrate Halloween and now because of this icon it feels like it's always Halloween. 3) The recipes aren't easily accessible, don't show up in frequents. Edit: App has been uninstalled, cancelled. Thank you for the good you did!

Terrible for Singapore users. I found this app very difficult to use. Extremely frustrated with the manual entry requirements. Too complicated and time-consuming.

Doesn't work anymore after update, what am I paying for then? edit.: still the same issues and even more popups. getting into the app takes longer than the tracking itself. it's extremely annoying. edit.: 26. you need to click through 26 popups before you can track if you don't buy the premium. that's ridiculous

This app has gotten so greedy and annoying. Youre telling me I have to buy Pro to track calories for tomorrow? Everytime I put my meal in I have to watch an animation then get an ad.
